pontac-manor-hotel-restaurant.text: We had breakfast a number of times at peaches and cream and really enjoyed the quality of food as as well as the location.

pontac-manor-hotel-restaurant.author: Linda du Toit-Snyman
pontac-manor-hotel-restaurant.text: Had Father's day lunch Service was great

du-vin-restaurant.author: Liesl Leonard Kroutz
du-vin-restaurant.text: My husband and I did the sushi and wine pairing followed by some additional sushi from the menu. We were served by Okuhle who was professional, knowledgeable and provided excellent service. The setting was calming and picturesque and the restaurant was quiet, with only a few other diners—we visited on a Friday between 12:00 and 14:00.

du-vin-restaurant.author: Guppy Fraser
du-vin-restaurant.text: Had a lovely afternoon lunch, four of us. Easy to find, plenty of parking, some with shade. Wheelchair ♿️ friendly. Has curio shop and wine making tours. We went for the food and where not disappointed.

du-vin-restaurant.author: Joni Vally
du-vin-restaurant.text: Great place for kids with enclosed jungle gym under the trees. There is also a putt-putt course... thinknit is about R30 pp to play. Lovely views... Friendly service although it was a tad bit on the slow side. Would recommend going for a nice slow afternoon.

lievland-caf.author: Miryam Kaniki
lievland-caf.text: Lovely place with a great atmosphere. We were warmly welcomed and really enjoyed the starters, which made for a promising start to the experience. Unfortunately, things took a turn when our mains arrived. I ordered the Sunday roast, but it came out cold and was quite underwhelming.

lievland-caf.author: Clem Weld Forester
lievland-caf.text: Just wanted to write a quick review to say that we randomly found this cafe during our stay in stellanbosh and went there EVERYDAY for breakfast as it was just outstanding. The coffee and food - we tried as much of the menu as we could - was just amazing, incredible location and surroundings, very attentive staff and so reasonable.

lievland-caf.author: Lisa Harlow
lievland-caf.text: Very tasty bistro style food, I chose the Parisian Gnocchi, with artichoke, tomato and aubergine. They were very good, light and fluffy gnocchi I chose a glass of the rosé to go with my lunch on a very warm day It was quite noisy as there were some big groups in, but I think that was unusual It’s worth going for a wine tasting either befo…

fyre-dining.author: Christophe Beauman
fyre-dining.text: Set in such a wonderful setting, beautifully designed. We arrived and greeted with a welcome glass of wine from which we glided into a perfect evening. I was astounded to be honest just how many meals were flying out of the kitchen. Service was spot on and we had a perfectly steak. Highly recommend.

fyre-dining.author: Christine de Valence
fyre-dining.text: This was unbelievable - from the moment we walked up to the entrance to the last glass of wine. The service was impeccable and the atmosphere left me wanting to stay for more. The food - DELICIOUS. Authentic South African flare. We tried two or three different items and each was so well prepared - we had to tell the chef.

fyre-dining.author: Zelna Pelser
fyre-dining.text: Was lucky enough to watch Roan Ash in person at FYRE dining. What an experience. Really one of the most special events. The venue is absolutely exquisite. The event was very chilled and the platters were divine. Staff is friendly and very attentive. Loved the intimate setting and would absolutely recommend this 💯.

blueberry-bar.author: Austin Dajon Naidoo
blueberry-bar.text: Amazing experience, starts of with a tractor ride which takes you to an area where there's more than enough blueberries for you to pick. They sell coffee at the kiosk and other refreshments however there is no actual food here. Would suggest maybe selling scones with their blueberry jam it would definitely be a hit.

blueberry-bar.author: Petrus Delport
blueberry-bar.text: Excellent outing with the kids to pick and taste some blueberries. Facilities are nice and clean and the tractor / trailer ride was a bonus for the kids

blueberry-bar.author: JB Bekker
blueberry-bar.text: Our visit to Blueberry Bar was an excellent experience from start to finish. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, making us feel at home immediately. We had the delightful opportunity to make blueberry jam from fresh blueberries, which was a fun and memorable activity.

caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.author: Chris Taylor
caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.text: My wife and I came through for a peaceful breakfast. We were greeted by a very friendly hostess. We ordered some tea and coffee which came out very quickly. Our waitress was friendly and professional. We both had the overnight oats and it was amazing. Overall vibe and ambience was fantastic. Value for money was excellent.

caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.author: Olaf Ransome
caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.text: Go for Saturday steak night. This was the best meat we have had in South Africa since we arrived in February, Kudos to Adriano, Angelo and the whole team> My wife tried the rib-eye, I took the fillet. Both excellent. Veggis suepr, fries so tasty you did not want ketchup and mayo.

caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.author: Timofey
caf-del-sol-val-de-vie-estate.text: Visited Sela Vie three times and I’m truly impressed with the exceptional quality of the food. Every ingredient bursts with amazing flavors. The staff is friendly and attentive, adding to the experience. And the espresso? Simply great!

chez-shiraz.author: Andre Griffin
chez-shiraz.text: Great customer service with delicious food. This restaurant is a must place to visit if you love quality food and clean facility.

chez-shiraz.author: Brian Porter
chez-shiraz.text: We visited as a party of four on a Wednesday evening. What a gorgeous setting! The restaurant was reasonably full, and there were one or two large groups. It was decided to order four starters and four main courses in order for everyone to sample the offerings.

chez-shiraz.author: Graham Taylor
chez-shiraz.text: Such an amazing view of the golf course. Service is great

ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.author: BV
ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.text: Ischia is a fantastic dining experience in Stellenbosch. Every dish was beautifully presented and packed with flavour. The ravioli was rich and perfectly cooked, and the cinnamon gelato was simply superb—the perfect ending to the meal.

ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.author: Johan du Preez
ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.text: We discovered Ischia Restaurant Stellenbosch while looking for a spot for a light lunch – and it turned out to be a genuinely special experience. The food is authentically Italian, made with quality ingredients, and both fresh and flavourful.

ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.author: Longest Drive
ischia-restaurant-stellenbosch.text: From the moment we arrived at Ischia Restaurant Stellenbosch, it felt like stepping straight into a modern Italian dream right here in the Cape Winelands. The atmosphere strikes the perfect balance between elegant and relaxed — chic Mediterranean vibes with warm hospitality that makes you feel instantly at ease.

louiesenhof-wines.author: Tim Crombie
louiesenhof-wines.text: Wine tasting: Pretty and quaint. Our group thoroughly enjoyed our wine tasting. Our server was helpful and friendly. We got to to taste for free. We thought their wines were delicious and most of us bought a bottle or two. Not as fancy as other estates but a welcome change from the usual pompousness.

louiesenhof-wines.author: Matthew Burns
louiesenhof-wines.text: We really enjoyed the wine tasting experience at this winery. We went late on a Sunday, so we were one of two tables sampling. The staff waited on us hand and foot. That combined with the tasty wines "forced" us to buy some bottles. Not as expensive as other wineries.

louiesenhof-wines.author: Andre Hart (Hart Shots Photography)
louiesenhof-wines.text: Best pot still brandy I have ever tasted. The port is also first class and the wine follows suit. you simply have to stop here for a tasting. Areal hidden gem.

plat-coffee-bottelary.author: John Citral
plat-coffee-bottelary.text: I never thought I would be writing a negative review about a Plato branch, as I have always enjoyed the brand, but this was a genuinely disappointing experience. Unfortunately, this is also the Plato closest to my home. I visited early one morning and was surprised by the complete lack of customer service.

plat-coffee-bottelary.author: Greta Duvenage
plat-coffee-bottelary.text: Love this spot! The baristas were super friendly and hyped us up for the long drive home after the holiday—such a great start to the day. We ordered a Flat White and a Platō, and both were excellent as always.

plat-coffee-bottelary.author: Michael Saunders
plat-coffee-bottelary.text: Stopped off on opening day and was blown away by the friendliness of the folk and baristas. The quirky, pleasantly fresh experience, and most importantly, the time and care taken to create the perfect, custom brew. We use any excuse under the sun to drop by and enjoy the people, place and product. Well done Platō Coffee Team.

lorsonis-at-the-salene.author: Joe Botha
lorsonis-at-the-salene.text: Just thirteen minutes from Stellenbosch and still feeling delightfully undiscovered, this place is a proper find. The views are genuinely stunning, the chicken salad is a standout, and the whole spot has that local-secret feel you don’t stumble on often.

lorsonis-at-the-salene.author: Saukat-Hussein Dudhia
lorsonis-at-the-salene.text: Enjoyed Mothers' Day lunch at Lorsoni's, which was the only venue which accepted a last minute booking for 4 of us. What a delightful setting with panoramic views of the Hottentot mountains. Service was first-rate, but the star of the show was the food on offer - we ordered lamb shank which just fell off the bone tender!

lorsonis-at-the-salene.author: Vlad McKeavic
lorsonis-at-the-salene.text: I had the best sirloin steak here that I’ve ever had. Perfectly cooked, incredibly tender, and full of flavor. The quality of the meat was outstanding and you could really taste it. On top of that, the service was phenomenal friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming.

mooiberge-farmstall.author: Will Smit
mooiberge-farmstall.text: The farm stall offers a great variety of preserved vegetables and fruit, including the fresh, in-season strawberries it is well known for. The wine and spirits collection is just as impressive. Prices are a bit on the high side, but the quality is good. You will still pick up a few bargains, especially among the wines.

mooiberge-farmstall.author: Marié-Louise Kriel
mooiberge-farmstall.text: Writing Very nice farm stall. However, the ladies’ restroom should be in a much better condition. If customers are expected to pay to use the facilities, one would reasonably expect them to be clean and well maintained.

mooiberge-farmstall.author: Rick R
mooiberge-farmstall.text: Review is about the store. They have some lovely items on sale. The wine section has a very good selection. You can find really old vintages here. The stall also sells good jam and dried fruits. The walnuts are the best buy. Excellent quality. But not always available. I must also add that the staff are not the best.

babylonstoren-wine-estate.author: Yaron Koren
babylonstoren-wine-estate.text: A magical place and a very interesting tour guided by Kobus who was exceptionally good! The estate is a large agricultural farm, run with impressive organization, almost like a well-coordinated factory where everything works in harmony. Highly recommended.

babylonstoren-wine-estate.author: Tanz
babylonstoren-wine-estate.text: Absolutely adore this place. Although, not a cheap venue, what is on offer is worth the cost! The "reallness" of this working farm is very appealling. They have bees, grow all kinds of herbs, vegetable and fruit. They have water buffalo (fresh ice-cream and cheese to buy) and grapes (delish wines).

babylonstoren-wine-estate.author: Erin Berryman
babylonstoren-wine-estate.text: Babylonstoren is unlike any winery we’ve ever been to. The gardens, variety of dining, service was all impeccable. Could easily spend the whole day there. Bit away from other wineries we were going to but it was well worth the detour.

j-c-le-roux.author: Antonie Kempen
j-c-le-roux.text: It was such an experience. The staff was so professional and very friendly. The staff really know their stuff. It was affordable and very classy. I cant stop telling friend and family about our experience. I absolutely loved every single second. Can't wait to visit again. Would love to try the biltong tasting 😋

j-c-le-roux.author: Kelly Roman
j-c-le-roux.text: Drinks was good, tasting was good, music and atmosphere was mellow and nice. The restaurant okay..... Besides the drinks which amazingly yummy. I think if there was a little bit of mellow vibes music playing upstairs. Maybe we would've spent and stayed longer. The flies oh mother nature was tricky.

j-c-le-roux.author: Dwighmegon Balie
j-c-le-roux.text: I had a great experience tasting champagne with chocolate. I would highly recommend people to experience this wonderful wine tasting.

devonvale-golf-wine-estate.author: Emihle Hempe
devonvale-golf-wine-estate.text: First time promoting at the golf course and I had the best time. The atmosphere, views, the staff and all the jokes made by the golfers added to the wonderful experience. I will definitely visit the place again for breakfast by myself or a group of friends.

devonvale-golf-wine-estate.author: Mamela Sodzeme
devonvale-golf-wine-estate.text: We visited the restaurant and I enjoyed my food alot. Our waitress was Yolokazi and she gave us an amazing service. Beautiful

devonvale-golf-wine-estate.author: Sahar Antoine
devonvale-golf-wine-estate.text: Beautiful golf course. ⛳️🏌️ It can be even play by beginner. You have a lot of springbox walking around you while you play. This is amazing Staff are very friendly and they do they best to find a tee time. The fairways are in excellent shape. Go go go play ⛳️⛳️⛳️⛳️

rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.author: David Cacik
rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.text: Walking through the door of Rust en Vrede you can feel and see a bit of more noblesse than other wineries in the area. The service, quality and the wines live up to the initial expectations. Simple and powerful - same as the wine variety selection (syrah, cabsav, merlot only).

rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.author: sIIcAnton
rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.text: Our connection to Rust en Vrede is very special. We have been there twice now and it never ceases to amaze us. The wines are obviously superb, but the restaurant itself is a masterpiece. Our evenings were perfect and we are already counting down to our next visit in November. This place is a must for anyone visiting South Africa.

rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.author: Cindy Ng
rust-en-vrede-wine-estate.text: Absolutely stunning setting with excellent wine and the friendliest staff. The winemakers lunch is absolutely delightful enjoying a nice meal outside under the trees. Perfectly cooked steak or salmon that comes with a paired glass of wine. A must visit when you are in Stellenbosch!

vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.author: Ewan Cranwell
vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.text: Siran gave us unbelievable service even though he was new to the job!! If he hadn’t told us, we would’ve thought he’d been there for years! Very knowledgable, and really took his time to tell us stories about each wine. The setting is simply stunning.

vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.author: Milly Tuck
vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.text: We couldn’t have asked for a better wedding venue. From the beautiful rooms and stunning grounds to the picturesque backdrops for our photos, everything was perfect. The staff were incredible throughout the day. Our drinks were never empty, the banter never stopped, and nothing was too much trouble.

vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.author: michelle meehan
vrede-en-lust-wine-estate.text: We chose Vrede en Lust Wine Estate as our wedding venue, and I truly cannot thank Juanne and the entire team enough for creating the most incredible three-night retreat for us and our guests. I first visited the venue with Juanne a year before our wedding, and it completely lived up to — and exceeded — the dream.

haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.author: Kyla Phyfer
haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.text: A lovely birthday lunch was enjoyed thanks to the incredible atmosphere, exceptional food and drinks, and wonderful service at Haute Cabrière. Having visited countless times before, it still somehow feels like a new experience with every visit because of the consistently high standard, attention to detail, and level of care put into every…

haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.author: Bonginhlanhla Gcabashe
haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.text: From the moment we stepped through the doors, the hospitality and attention to detail were immediately evident. The team welcomed us warmly, guided us through the process effortlessly, and created an atmosphere that felt relaxed and inviting from the very beginning.

haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.author: Helen Broekmann
haute-cabri-re-the-home-of-pierre-jourda.text: Ashwill was a lovely host at out tasting. We tasted the Shades of Chardonnay and were given generous amounts of each. The silk nectar bubbly is sublime. We were a little disappointed that our roasted nuts which we ordered extra were far from fresh - or they had been left out on a counter for hours and had gone soft.

kanu-wines.author: Lucette Blom
kanu-wines.text: I came here for a wine tasting. You get value for money as the person who helped us was very generous with the sparking and normal wines to the extent we had to stop him pouring more. 😆 We did the wine tasting in the cellar and that was special.

kanu-wines.author: Jacolize Grobler
kanu-wines.text: Very nice place to visit. Very friendly waitress that served us. Cosy atmosphere in the cellar for wine tasting. It was just unfortunate that we couldn't eat something at the restaurant ( they are seperate from the winery)

kanu-wines.author: Joseph Moses
kanu-wines.text: Saturday we went for wine tasting and the experience was just amazing. Good quality wines with a great atmosphere and sitting inside the old cement "tanks" makes the experience just so much more special. Great pricing too

ernie-els-wines.author: Stefan Coetzee
ernie-els-wines.text: Absolutely incredible! It was my mother's 89th birthday, and we couldn't have asked for a better experience. Great service (what a wonderful waitress we had!), great food, and of course breath-taking surroundings. The bill did not break the bank either. Astonishingly beautiful place.

ernie-els-wines.author: Neil Higgs
ernie-els-wines.text: What an exceptional experience. Right from the warm and amazing welcome, to the impeccably presented and delicious dishes, beautifully served by our waitress, to the amazing venue and view, it was a memorable lunch. Our dietary needs were ascertained and totally respected, the coffee to round off was the best I gave had in a restaurant.

ernie-els-wines.author: Jakub Veber
ernie-els-wines.text: We had great time at Ernie Els. Great wines, great food, amazing views and service. We first visited three years ago and again during last holidays. And I cannot wait to visit again.

peter-falke-wines.author: Tanz
peter-falke-wines.text: Interesting and creative venue. Unfortunately we could not take full advantage of the stunning outside area or kiddies playground. Food was yummy! Had their lamb shank and cheesecake. The staff were amazing, caring and helpful, even with our very busy little one.

peter-falke-wines.author: Oydin Ali
peter-falke-wines.text: It’s so beautiful absolutely gorgeous outdoor sitting you get 360 views And wine was great toooo absolutely you should visit

peter-falke-wines.author: Jaco
peter-falke-wines.text: Absolutely an amazing estate. The wine tasting is a nice and the cheese and meat platter to share is lovely.

waterford-estate.author: Heidi Hudson-Bennett
waterford-estate.text: Spent the most incredible time at Waterford over the last 3 days. Each experience was amazing. The attention to detail and excellent service made it a wonderful getaway. The staff are all friendly and very knowledgeable about all aspects of the wine and farm life.

waterford-estate.author: Laura-Lee Gillion
waterford-estate.text: This was one of the best experiences I’ve had at a wine farm. The food was delicious and satiating. The atmosphere is lovely and welcoming and the staff are amazing, attentive and professional. I’d like to personally thank Shaun as well as our lovely waiter Evan. Because of them I’d definitely be back here the next chance I get!

waterford-estate.author: Mariska De Wet
waterford-estate.text: We enjoyed the Cellar Collection wine tasting, which was superb as well as a delicious platter from their SALT Restaurant. Myburgh's service was excellent and Dylan a gracious host. The rain came pouring down, but the fire and welcoming atmosphere made us feel right at home.

le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.author: Keryn Crawcour | Her Heiness
le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.text: Wow wow and wow!!! Myself and my boyfriend visited Orangerie at Le Lude this past weekend and we both agreed that we haven’t received such amazing service in ages. The food was honestly top notch, the quality and flavour was outstanding. Highly recommend the burrata and the mushroom tortellini (I’m vegetarian).

le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.author: Barbara Mackenzie
le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.text: What a stunning estate. I went their with friends for an easy champagne tasting. Didn’t make a reservation and service staff was very helpful with seating us. Tasting was informative and bubbly definitely one of the best around in the WP. Love they rosé bubbly. Good price for an outstanding quality.

le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.author: Valerie Amanda
le-lude-estate-and-orangerie-restaurant.text: Really beautiful restaurant worth a visit while you’re in Franschoek. We had the bubbles which were lovely. The starters were all delicious but the mains were less impressive. Friendly / helpful service as well.

webersburg-wine-estate.author: Dan K.
webersburg-wine-estate.text: Had a wine tasting and light lunch in the cellar. Host was extremely knowledgeable and well spoken. I'm not a wine drinker, decided since I was here I would give them a try and the wines were all very good and food pairings exceptional. Everyone that worked here went above and beyond in accommodating everyone's questions and needs.

webersburg-wine-estate.author: Cephas Machiri
webersburg-wine-estate.text: We had the pleasure of visiting Webersburg Wine Estate for a wedding, and it was truly an unforgettable experience. The estate is absolutely beautiful, with breathtaking scenery all around and such a peaceful, relaxing atmosphere. The weather was perfect, which made the experience even more special.

webersburg-wine-estate.author: Pieter Van Wyk
webersburg-wine-estate.text: Beautiful setting with a nice mountain backdrop and lawn rolling down to a small dam with geese. It's an classic old farm with old and new architecture combined into a tasteful balance. The red wine is exceptional and exceeds world standards. I highly recommend.

guardian-peak-winery-grill.author: Neil Higgs
guardian-peak-winery-grill.text: This is truly an amazing restaurant. We have been several times, always outstanding. Food is beyond brilliant, service beyond reproach. Management is very hands on, which shows. The staff have been trained to talk about the Estate and surrounds, about the wines here and in the area. It all adds to a wonderful experience.

guardian-peak-winery-grill.author: Andries Heyneke
guardian-peak-winery-grill.text: Guardian Peak Restaurant Jason gave outstanding service that really improved the experience. The lamb potpie was amazing—rich, comforting, and perfectly seasoned. The view and the calm surroundings are next level, making it a standout spot for great food and peaceful vibes. Highly recommended for a memorable visit.

guardian-peak-winery-grill.author: Constantia Cottages
guardian-peak-winery-grill.text: Unfortunately, we hadn't been to Guardian Peak for quite some time, which we deeply regretted after this visit. On 16th January 2026, we were able to sit outside on the deck and enjoy the breathtaking view of the valley.

la-petite-ferme.author: Lauren Esch
la-petite-ferme.text: I booked a surprise stay for my husband and I for our 21st wedding anniversary, and La Petite Ferme did not disappoint. It’s one of the most memorable, romantic stays we’ve ever had. The staff are exceptional (thank you Levi!!!!!, Wisdom, Janine and Wanda). The wine tasting is above and beyond. The food absolutely exceptional.

la-petite-ferme.author: Danielle
la-petite-ferme.text: We had an incredible birthday lunch here in January. The staff was exceptional, going above and beyond to make us feel welcome. The food was exceptional, especially the tasting menu. They even custom-made a chocolate cake for the birthday, which was incredible. The wine selection was also amazing, and the views were breathtaking.

la-petite-ferme.author: Robert Bloomberg
la-petite-ferme.text: Great 3 night stay. Vineyard suites are excellent and well situated with magnificent views. Fantastic breakfasts. Top staff. Beautiful and peaceful location. Highly recommended

laborie-wine-estate.author: Sandra Mukasa
laborie-wine-estate.text: Went to the Stoep restaurant at Laborie wine estate. Beautiful place. Nice vibe. Kiddies play area. Not too far from Cape Town. Worth the drive.

laborie-wine-estate.author: Savannah-lee Pitzer
laborie-wine-estate.text: I stayed at Laborie after a wedding at their venue. The room was gorgeous with the most beautiful view of the vineyards. The included breakfast was unlimited and divine! The staff were so lovely and easy assisted me the next day to book a last minute extra night in my room. I’d go every year

laborie-wine-estate.author: SA CCJ
laborie-wine-estate.text: Laborie Heritage Estate is one of the crown jewels of the Paarl wine region. While estates like Boschendal represent the grand scale of Stellenbosch, Laborie offers a more intimate, refined atmosphere that beautifully preserves its French Huguenot heritage. 1. Historical Background: A Taste of France * Established: 1691.

nuudo-luxury-express-spa.author: Anneke Kotze
nuudo-luxury-express-spa.text: The transition from good to excellent is rarely about a single massive leap, but rather a consistent, disciplined, and often subtle shift in mindset and action. Good is characterized by meeting standards, whereas excellent is characterized by exceeding them through continuous improvement, high-level discipline, and a focus on the "extra"…

nuudo-luxury-express-spa.author: Emma van Damme
nuudo-luxury-express-spa.text: Most incredible luxury pedi experience! Most amazing treat ever. Highly recommend if you need a spoil and can splurge a bit. The best birthday gift I received!

nuudo-luxury-express-spa.author: Cathy Jordaan
nuudo-luxury-express-spa.text: This was the most wonderful pedicure experience I have ever had, absolutely amazing! The atmosphere is beautiful and calming, and the overall experience was exceptional. The only small suggestion would be to consider softer, more relaxing spa-style music to enhance the tranquil setting.

morgenster-estate.author: Tessa De Necker
morgenster-estate.text: Spectacular views, great wine and a magical experience tasting olive oil. Highly recommended for everyone's bucketlist. Seating on the verandah with a view like that on a Sunday afternoon is perfect. Staff very friendly and knowledgeable.

morgenster-estate.author: Mares Adventures
morgenster-estate.text: One of the nicest wine tastings we have done. The surroundings and ambience, the staff are lovely, the platter was well presented and the wine selection truly worth the experience. We will definitely be going back

morgenster-estate.author: Yvette Vandenijssel
morgenster-estate.text: excellent, honest, delicious food. not pretentious, and big portions. good value for money, actually i think the prices are too low. very very friendly personel. we have been coming for more than 15 years and never been disappointed. their rating should be towards 4.5 !

meulwater-botanical-garden.author: Elizabeth O'Rourke
meulwater-botanical-garden.text: Easy hike for beginners at Paarl Rock we park outside resort and did the walk at no charge. If you have pensionars with you pay the extra fee and let them drive closer to the Paarl rock to just wait there and take memorable photos

meulwater-botanical-garden.author: Jean Luc Boucher
meulwater-botanical-garden.text: Beautiful location. Its quiet and serene, property well kept. Plenty of shade and choice to pick. Braai spot. No baboons to steal your stuff either! Just be aware there is 6km of gravel to travel over though.

meulwater-botanical-garden.author: david kaye
meulwater-botanical-garden.text: Beautiful sight with magnificent views overlooking the Paarl valley. There are plenty of braai areas with clean public toilet facilities. There are many walking paths, and climbing up Paarl Rock was a stunning experience. Very popular, especially on weekends and public holidays.

blaauwklippen-tasting-house.author: David Cacik
blaauwklippen-tasting-house.text: Probably the most kid friendly winery in stellenbosch! Plenty of kid amenities including 2 massive playgrounds and a pumptrack. Wines are excellent too - did the 6 glass tasting. Pizza was alright.

blaauwklippen-tasting-house.author: Emma Schmid
blaauwklippen-tasting-house.text: We did a wine tasting at Blaauwklippen today and it was a great experience! It being the middle of the week definitely meant that it was a lot quieter than on a weekend which was really nice, we didn’t have a booking and there was still plenty of space.

blaauwklippen-tasting-house.author: Natalie Smith
blaauwklippen-tasting-house.text: We stayed at Blaauwklippen Manor House in a deluxe room over Valentine’s weekend, and unfortunately, the experience fell far short of expectations—especially given the high price point. The beautiful restoration and renovation of the historic Manor House is truly impressive.

kleine-zalze-wine-estate.author: Ryan Hultzer
kleine-zalze-wine-estate.text: Fantastic wines and really enjoyable lunch experience. Reasonably priced for the value too.

kleine-zalze-wine-estate.author: Wandile Ngwenya
kleine-zalze-wine-estate.text: I always drink their wine at home so it was very exciting to do restaurant week with them. When we arrived, we were greeted with a glass of MCC and a beautiful view of the estate. The options on the menu were plenty and they all looked delicious. The dishes were well presented and were even more delicious than they looked.

kleine-zalze-wine-estate.author: Stefan Bussels
kleine-zalze-wine-estate.text: Where else to combine an exquisite meal with beautiful wines? What Zalze does very well is serving full, tasty dishes that have a high end feel, but at the same time are seemingly based in fundamental flavours. They pair them with their own wines, obviously tasty in their own right.

bellevue-wine-estate.author: Alricia Du Toit
bellevue-wine-estate.text: Gorgeous estate with friendly and knowledgeable staff. The wines are exceptional and the cheese and charcuterie platter was absolutely delicious. It is also dog friendly, which means we could bring our dog with to enjoy the experience.

bellevue-wine-estate.author: The Wine Girl Cape Town
bellevue-wine-estate.text: Bellevue has relocated the tasting room to the manor house and offer some great sharing platters and Pinotage 😍 What I love about this winery is that they offer three different Pinotage bottles suited for different palates. They have a super history when it comes to Pinotage!

bellevue-wine-estate.author: Theis Solling
bellevue-wine-estate.text: Pinotage pinotage pinotage - absolutely an amazing wine. It's sadly not well-known in Europe. Yet...... 5 🌟 to Johan for explaining the history behind the grape 🙏 The knowledge and the enthusiasm was absolutely 💯 Unparalleled anywhere we have been

simonsvlei-wines.author: Lise-Mari Gillespie
simonsvlei-wines.text: Lovely spot for lunch and having the kids play. There is an additional area/restaurant where it's more of a beer garden setup with live music and closer to the kids section. The Oxtail lasagne is a MUST try, big pieces of soft meat layered in cheesy pasta sheets in a perfect size portion.

simonsvlei-wines.author: T
simonsvlei-wines.text: Amazing art display at the moment in Feb 26, ! Pet friendly on wime tasting area, a feast! Leather couches and fire places 🔥 Makes it extra special.

simonsvlei-wines.author: Michael Smith
simonsvlei-wines.text: Our visit to Simonsvlei was nothing short of amazing. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth — and a special shout-out goes to Neil, who was so, so friendly and made the entire experience feel personal and unforgettable.

stellenrust-wine-estate.author: Madeline Mckernan
stellenrust-wine-estate.text: Absolutely love this winery! It was probably my favorite one I went to! The pinotage is excellent. I brought some back to friends and they also loved it! Would recommend. The picnic basket is a great value and the wine and cake pairing is a unique tasting that we really enjoyed.

stellenrust-wine-estate.author: Zoie Does Things
stellenrust-wine-estate.text: Had a quick tasting here the other day. Was pleasantly surprised by this year’s wine. They blew us away. The tasting room is cosy (can do with a bit of a decor update) and inviting and staff are really friendly.

stellenrust-wine-estate.author: Dewald Reinke
stellenrust-wine-estate.text: I recently attended a work function at Stellenrust Wine Estate and had a generally good time. The outdoor area is beautiful, with lush green lawns, gardens, water features, and vineyards with mountains as a backdrop. Their indoor space is equally impressive, featuring a decent bar area and a spacious hall perfect for hosting events.

anura-vineyards.author: Peter Chong
anura-vineyards.text: Stunning and spacious gem in Klapmuts. Stylish wine tasting venue. Amazing variety of wines mainly for export but affordable. Chardonnay is the best white and all their reds and blends are of top quality. Malbec is on par with Neethlingshof and the 2 different pinotage does showcase our South African heritage.

anura-vineyards.author: Mary-Lee du Plessis
anura-vineyards.text: Total bliss, my kind of place and I can't wait to go back! If you're looking for top notch service, fewer crowds, stunning views and just an all round perfect day out then this is for you! Every single person working on the farm was spledid, courteous and happy to help. The Legato is fabulous. The Sauvignon Blanc brilliant.

anura-vineyards.author: Ben Mouton
anura-vineyards.text: This was a highlight for my girlfriend and I on Valentine’s Day! Very friendly security at the gate and beautiful scenery driving to the parking. When we stopped we walked to the Bistro and staff gladly helped to show us around, their coffee is delicious! We had a scrumptious lunch and a big thank you to Zane our waiter!

marianne-wine-estate.author: Pierre Dupas
marianne-wine-estate.text: What a nice place to stay 😀 We spent a full week at Marianne Wine Estate and had an absolutely wonderful stay. The estate is stunning, the rooms are beautiful and very comfortable, and the views are simply breathtaking. The wines are excellent, and both the welcome and service were flawless from start to finish.

marianne-wine-estate.author: Kendra Keels
marianne-wine-estate.text: We only went for the wine tasting. It was a beautiful drive into the winery. The grounds are gorgeous. We sat on the patio and the atmosphere was awesome. We started with the white wines and then a meat board was brought out for each meat to accompany each red wine. It was excellent! We enjoyed the pairings.

marianne-wine-estate.author: David Schneider
marianne-wine-estate.text: Tasting experience here: top notch (but the wines are over-oaked and over-alcoholized--15. 5% for the flagship red blend) Staff: working so hard, fantastic people. Ownership: cutting corners all over the place at the expense of guest experience and their staff. We decided to splurge here for three nights as a family in two rooms.

fort-simon-wine-estate.author: Amelia Todkill
fort-simon-wine-estate.text: Had my wedding here 2 months ago, absolutely beautiful chapel and hall, gave the most beautiful rustic, raw and natural feeling. Prices are really reasonable and the staff were very friendly and helpful. Thank you Jorika for all your effort to make my day unforgettable! You guys are amazing :)

fort-simon-wine-estate.author: El-Chante Barnard
fort-simon-wine-estate.text: Very beautiful place! Peaceful and quiet. 3 stars for being HIGHLY over priced with their platters 👎🏻 R320 for a platter which serves 2. With a “variety of premium cured meats”, one would expect more than 4 pieces of salami on your platter.

fort-simon-wine-estate.author: Simoné Engelbrecht
fort-simon-wine-estate.text: Stunning estate. Lovely food. Awesome wine. An AMAZING place to visit. Very chilled vibes. I got married here in 2023 so it's a special place to my husband and I.

holden-manz-wine-estate.author: Shannon McGarrity
holden-manz-wine-estate.text: Absolutely stunning experience!! Simmie, the waitress for wine tasting was a gem. The Cab Franc was the best we have had. One of the owners, Mr. Holden, introduced himself and had a smile for days. We sat by the fire, soaked in the views, and had an amazing experience for one of our spontaneous stops for our 20th anniversary.

holden-manz-wine-estate.author: Tomas Savarell
holden-manz-wine-estate.text: We have made an unplanned stop at Holden Manz and stayed longer than expected. We have been greeted with a glass of rose and later enjoyed a wonderful crisp selection of whites. Charcuterie board was a fantastic compliment to our wines and the surroundings were like from a dream.

holden-manz-wine-estate.author: Kobus de Winnaar
holden-manz-wine-estate.text: Gershwan has been so incredibly from walking into the farm to having enjoying a welcoming wine tate. Make the time and come visit one of the greatest secrets of the weatern cape,Holden manz is a must go for high quality wines Enjoy the wine , the location and the service. It is excellent value for money.

backsberg-family-wines.author: Kirstin Wiedow
backsberg-family-wines.text: Cosy atmosphere with a roaring fire and attentive service. A bottle of the Chenin comes in at 95 ZAR which is super affordable and Sauvignon Blanc at just over 100 and very quaffable! Tried the cheese platter which was underwhelming at 190 ZAR.

backsberg-family-wines.author: Nina Naude
backsberg-family-wines.text: What an amazing place! Difficult to find as it is very hidden behind Franschhoek Cellars. Once there, you have this amazing view, incredible wines and wonderful staff. Awesome wine pairing and well worth a visit... will be back for sure. Thanks, Clarien... ❣️❣️🍷🍷

backsberg-family-wines.author: dirk heyns
backsberg-family-wines.text: Service is great, wine was good and view is stunning would send anyone there for a wines tasting.... did not eat but the food that came out looked really yummy.

noble-hill.author: Suzie Allderman
noble-hill.text: The new menu has been designed to complement the Noble Hill wines - starters of Carrot Soup, Pickled Ginger, Toasted Pumpkin Seeds and the Pan Con Tomato were delish, and my main course of Chicken Marbella was divine - chef's kiss! Paired perfectly with their Simonskop Chenin Blanc 2025 from their premium range.

noble-hill.author: Devan Pulliah
noble-hill.text: The setting is beautiful with trees and a lovely lake...we went for brunch as breakfast ends at 11 and lunch starts thereafter...the service is friendly with undercover and under the trees seating areas...we chose to sit under the trees...there breakfast menu is not that big & reasonable priced with a Mexican theme...all either veggies or…

noble-hill.author: Eleanora Bresler
noble-hill.text: We sat in the beautiful garden setting under the trees. Tammy, our waitress was great! Super helpful and efficient. Guacamole made in faint if you is a fun touch and delicious. The food is good. Carrot cake average. Food prices are a bit high.

dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.author: Lynn Coenen
dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.text: After spending three summers here, we can’t believe no one told us about this winery sooner! We only discovered it thanks to a local recommendation — and we’re so grateful we did. The cellar tour was incredibly personal, engaging, and genuinely inspiring.

dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.author: Anlé Britz
dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.text: Brownie and bubbles for breakfast? Absolutely a must. The brownie & MCC pairing is pure joy - while the chocolate pairing is divine too, the brownies definitely steal the show. The service is top-notch, and I mean… just look at that view. One of those places where the scenery is just as intoxicating as what’s in your glass.

dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.author: James Dooms
dieu-donn-vineyards-cellar-tasting-room.text: Great views and great sparking wines! We came here as part of the wine tram experience (pink line) and decided to do the sparkling wine with brownie pairing. It all matched great with the views and overall experience. The staff were very friendly and the pairing was very good.

klein-goederust-wine-farm.author: Shane Louw
klein-goederust-wine-farm.text: I had the great privilege and pleasure of attending Klein Goederust last week. What a legendary farm. The rich dynamic history of the farm is perfectly paired with their award winnings wines and I was transported from 1905 to the present day with a beautiful MCC NomaRoma.

klein-goederust-wine-farm.author: Pilar Johnson
klein-goederust-wine-farm.text: Lovely winery on the blue line of the tram. They offer pizza, cheese platter, or lamb with cheese platter. At the restaurant they have lamb BBQ on the spit, and a salad buffet along w their tasting. Beautiful! I liked their Syrah the most, stone fruits and spice.

klein-goederust-wine-farm.author: Elena T.
klein-goederust-wine-farm.text: This was my favourite stop on the Franschhoek Wine Tram blue route. The building and wine tasting room are beautiful — spacious, elegant, and set against a stunning backdrop. It’s a wonderful place to relax and really enjoy the surroundings while tasting their wines.

thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.author: Elmarie De Villiers
thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.text: My husband and I went for a back, neck and shoulder massage. We had a wonderful time. The message was great! We are well pleased with our experience.

thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.author: Carol Liebrecht
thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.text: Thank you for the feedback, I am still waiting for the call from your team? This Spa is so dated and tired, I was there is 2010 and there has not been an upgrade since. I arrived to a miserable receptionist who was not welcoming. Put in a room where all I could hear were the cars speeding past.

thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.author: Nicole Pretorius
thai-sabai-day-spa-skin-clinic.text: My fiance and I came to Thai Sabai for a quick neck massage after our flight from London. The therapists did a brilliant job. A few days later I went to have my nails done for my wedding, and they did a beautiful job.

louisvale-wines.author: Tammy Joy Hendriks
louisvale-wines.text: I honestly cannot thank the team at Louisvale enough. From start to finish, the experience was nothing short of exceptional. Having hosted my own bridal shower here two years ago, it felt incredibly special to return and celebrate my close friend’s bridal shower at a venue that already holds such meaningful memories for me.

louisvale-wines.author: Paul Schoeman
louisvale-wines.text: Exquisite wine and service! Fantastic food. What a gem, we'll definitely be back. All the staff was friendly and special thanks to Harris and Mogamat.

louisvale-wines.author: michelle watling
louisvale-wines.text: Beautiful location. Attention to detail, and food excellent. We went for mother's day the day before the storm. Clever set menu to accommodate a full house and complimentary glass of rosé on arrival along with a rose and bottle of rosé for all mother's.

hartenberg-wine-estate.author: J J
hartenberg-wine-estate.text: My wife and I hosted our gender reveal at Hartenberg Wine Estate, and I was truly impressed by how professional and effortless the entire experience was. From the initial email correspondence to the day itself, everything was handled seamlessly.

hartenberg-wine-estate.author: Y Graves
hartenberg-wine-estate.text: Loved!!! Would hightly recommend. Had a lovely, lazy, quiet, spring sunday wine tasting and grazing platter. The staff where very attentive, welcoming and friendly. Our waiter (forgotten her name) was very very knowledgeable and gave us a an unforgettable tasting experience.

hartenberg-wine-estate.author: Beverly Harris-Schenz
hartenberg-wine-estate.text: Hartenberg Wine Estate offers a beautiful setting for lunch or dinner outside in the garden area with attentive service, good food and fine wine. However, this time on our 4th visit, I was disappointed in the so-called Grazing Platter, which was to include charcuterie.

plaisir-wine-estate.author: Olivia Gooderham
plaisir-wine-estate.text: We stayed here in April 2026 as a couple. We were blown away by the kindness and generosity of the host and staff. Special thank you to Gale who was so informed and couldn’t do enough to help. The rooms were beautiful and the pool was lovely. We did get down to the tasting room for breakfast, which was gorgeous.

plaisir-wine-estate.author: Кирилл Иванов
plaisir-wine-estate.text: Hi everyone. Today I had the opportunity to visit a wine tasting at the beautiful Plaisir wine estate. When I asked for a tasting focused only on red wines, specifically Shiraz, the waiter offered me a tasting for R300 and mentioned that it was advertised at the entrance (checked after nothing was at the entrance).

plaisir-wine-estate.author: Zoë Whare
plaisir-wine-estate.text: Firstly id like to mention Shaandré and her phenomenal service and knowledge on all that was served to us. I asked a little bit about the cheese and got the answers i was looking for. She is incredibly attentive and friendly. We had the cheeseboard for one. And wow! We split it between two, but what a surprise on portioning.

bacco-estate-winery.author: Jaunaé Cloete
bacco-estate-winery.text: Great food, amazing wine& service We had an absolutely incredible experience at the beautiful Bacco Wine Estate, and a huge part of that was thanks to our waiter, Winnie. She was incredibly friendly and attentive, truly prioritizing all of our needs throughout the afternoon.

bacco-estate-winery.author: TS Mpanza
bacco-estate-winery.text: My husband and I visited this wine farm after a spontaneous trip . We checked out from where we were staying the night before and early morning I asked if he knew of a wine farm around the area i haven’t been to and without hesitation he suggested this wine farm . And I was very impressed with his recommendation.

bacco-estate-winery.author: Oliver Petrides
bacco-estate-winery.text: This place is a gem. Spencer looked after us perfectly, he was super knowledgeable with all the wines we tasted and he was very welcoming. We came on the wine tram and didn’t leave as we were having such a nice time! Wines were great, food was delicious and the views were beautiful.

chamonix-wine-farm.author: Anlé Britz
chamonix-wine-farm.text: Here, wine is made to be adored, nature to be celebrated, and moments cherished over a corked bottle. We were spoiled with a private cellar tour, discovering a collection of their most treasured wines - a truly special memory we’ll carry forever. The service? Absolutely exceptional, thoughtful, and warm.

chamonix-wine-farm.author: Original T
chamonix-wine-farm.text: The greeting was miserable which is a shame as I brought my family there to experience my favourite wine. The chocolate pairing was not great, the wine we tried was vinegary and the chocolate was cheap and cold. I have been completely put off by the wine.

chamonix-wine-farm.author: Jessica G
chamonix-wine-farm.text: We had booked to stay at Chamonix for 2 nights and there was a mix up through Booking.com. The host handled it so well- took care of us for the one night that a room was available at Chamonix and booked us a beautiful hotel in town for the second night, and even escorted us to the new hotel to make sure everything was taken care of.

la-bri-wine-estate.author: Anlé Britz
la-bri-wine-estate.text: Bubbles, Chardonnay, Roussanne, Petit Verdot - all enjoyed with a view so beautiful it quietly melts your troubles away and officially announces the arrival of the weekend. The service was kind, warm, and wonderfully friendly, making the entire experience feel effortless and inviting.

la-bri-wine-estate.author: Anzett Viljoen
la-bri-wine-estate.text: Great views. Excellent wines. That's what I love about Franschhoek tram and visiting the various farms. You get to taste wines not normally on the shelves in shops. After tasting the lovely wine at La brie, I have ordered and they have delivered at my house x2 in Mpumalanga. Definitely worth a visit if you are in Franschhoek.

la-bri-wine-estate.author: val
la-bri-wine-estate.text: The wine tasting was really good, every wine we tried was out of this world and you could tell they actually care about what they’re making and the view is honestly incredible. The staff deserve a rating of 10 stars with how attentive they are and made the experience this fun! Such a beautiful spot, would definitely go back!

sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.author: S Hayat
sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.text: Absolutely amazing experience with my package. My therapist was highly skilled. All staff were professional and pleasant. Great heated indoor pool, warm jacuzzi and sauna. Probably one of the best massages I have ever had. Will definitely return.

sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.author: A. Nägeli
sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.text: We really enjoyed our stay at Santé Wellness Retreat and Spa. The estate is absolutely stunning and wonderfully peaceful. The spa treatments with Hazel and Lamla were a highlight. We did the half-day detox package (scrub, seaweed wrap, full body massage), and everything was performed very professionally and tailored to our needs.

sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.author: Margaret Kilfeather
sant-wellness-retreat-and-spa.text: I have just completed a fabulous 1/2 day Spa session at Santé Wellness Retreat and Spa. It included 3 fabulous treatments, access to the fantastic pools and relaxation areas, followed by a wonderful lunch. The staff were extraordinary, professional, highly skilled and extremely pleasant.

dornier-wine-estate.author: Nina Naude
dornier-wine-estate.text: Lovely wine estate with nice wines. The views are beautiful and the setting very serene. Chenin blanc and Malbec outstanding. Rovane did a great presentation. Maybe too much information, but good if you are interested in where the grapes grow and how long it was in barrels.

dornier-wine-estate.author: N.3.l. 3
dornier-wine-estate.text: We had the most amazing time at Dornier - thanks to Adeline, the whole experience was an all a-rounder! She explained each and every wine precisely. I’ll always come back for the best experience! Kind regards the whole wine crew 🤍🍷

dornier-wine-estate.author: Rudy Vetter
dornier-wine-estate.text: Great wines in a perfect symbiosis of culinary, architecture and arts. Great Pairings accompanied by a very delicious cheese & charcuterie board. Wines were well introduced and presented with style and knowledge.

rickety-bridge-estate.author: Original T
rickety-bridge-estate.text: Great place with a clean room and beautiful breakfast! The room was a decent size and the bathroom was fine. The only think I wish was the floors having more cover with matt because it was very cold. The room was also a bit cold but we were fine. The ground are very pretty.

rickety-bridge-estate.author: Rodrigo Barba
rickety-bridge-estate.text: We visited Rickety Bridge Estate as part of the Franschhoek Wine Tram experience and had a fantastic time. The estate offers a wonderful selection of wines, especially whites. We did the wine tasting with a friend and were warmly welcomed by Gema, who looked after us incredibly well, attentive and friendly.

rickety-bridge-estate.author: Tahli van Zyl
rickety-bridge-estate.text: Garcelle welcomed my mother and me with a wine tasting where the atmosphere felt truly special. From her expertise in wine to the art they are so passionate about, every moment was inspiring. Garcelle and Jenma even connected to help me with my social media!

annandale-wines.author: Gergely
annandale-wines.text: Probably the most classic old world style winery in the area nested in old cape dutch buildings. Oldschool in the best possible way. Not your conveyor belt industrial tasting. Can only recommend.

annandale-wines.author: Marsh Joubert
annandale-wines.text: Sneakily fit in Annandale right before closing on our last day in Stellenbosch. I fell in love with their 2020 Cavalier. Highly recommend it. Learned a few things from our knowledgeable wine guide. And the decades old spiderwebs in the tasting room is a great touch. Must return to try Bertus Basson’s new venture Toevlug.

annandale-wines.author: Dewald Reinke
annandale-wines.text: We recently visited the Annandale Wine Estate to celebrate a birthday and had an all-round good time. The event, because of weather, was held in a small room next to the main tasting area, but if the weather was better it would have been held on their beautiful lawn area.

glenwood-vineyards.author: Emms Mat
glenwood-vineyards.text: Great wine tasting experience. Bongs (without the muscles 💪) did an excellent job. Even accommodating our 6 year old with her soft drink and water tasting. She felt included. The whole experience was top class. The sushi 🍣 oh my, something worth a try. This is the place- should you wish for a quiet calm and serene environment.

glenwood-vineyards.author: Wayne Laubscher
glenwood-vineyards.text: Amazing experience. Best wine tasting experience we have ever had. Come here to get some superb wines, but you will meet people like Chris and Foster...who took us on a journey of delight. It was 100% memorable.

glenwood-vineyards.author: Lynne Livingston
glenwood-vineyards.text: We had the best time at Glenwood, I think your Chardonnay is the best I’ve ever tasted. Secondly Bongs who took the time to explain everything was absolutely brilliant. We didn’t have the Sushi as we were going for lunch elsewhere but next time I will definitely try it.

leeu-passant-winery.author: Lisa Harlow
leeu-passant-winery.text: One of the best wine farms to visit in South Africa, especially if you are interested in wine. You can taste the whole Mullineux range, including their top wines. These are some of the best wines available in SA. Staff are very knowledgeable and it is a beautiful setting You must book. I have been turned away at the boom gate before.

leeu-passant-winery.author: Sahar Antoine
leeu-passant-winery.text: The beauty of Leeu Pasant Winery left me completely speechless. I visited many arount the world but this one is truly magnificent. The wine is also excellent. If you come to Cape Town and u love one youarent allow to miss this place. Staff are very professional and give us so many information during the tasting.

leeu-passant-winery.author: Kezli Visser
leeu-passant-winery.text: A stunning estate with beautiful surroundings, a cozy wine tasting room and delicious wine. Victor’s knowledge about the wine and estate was insightful, and his friendly personality made for an enjoyable tasting experience. Tasting wine from such old vines set it apart from other farms we’d visited during the day.

mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.author: Natasha Roux
mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.text: This was the second time I hosted my birthday party with Mooiplaas. Two years ago, we had their picnic baskets, and they were amazing. This year, we had their cheese platters, and everyone was so impressed! Once again, the team went out of their way to ensure we were comfortable and happy. The wine was great!

mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.author: Martin
mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.text: Went to Mooiplaas to celebrate my wife's birthday with a few close friends and it was lovely. We did a cheese board picnic and the cheeseboards were filling and affordable. The wine is very affordable and there is quite a large range to suit everyone's taste.

mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.author: Natalie Lincoln
mooiplaas-wine-estate-and-private-nature.text: I had a wonderful wine tasting experience - Nico is knowledgeable and interesting, and the wines are delicious, with a large variety. A fantastic family farm you shouldn’t miss.

kaapzicht-wine-estate.author: Tracy Grace
kaapzicht-wine-estate.text: Popped into Kaapzicht Wine Estate for an impromptu wine tasting on Saturday morning. Pleasantly surprised with the amazing upgrade that's taken place since our last visit. Was served a welcome drink upon arrival and made comfortable at a table of our choice.

kaapzicht-wine-estate.author: JB Bekker
kaapzicht-wine-estate.text: Kaapzicht is a wine farm that immediately makes you feel welcome, offering a beautiful blend of authentic farm charm and rich family heritage. The estate is well maintained, with breathtaking vineyard views and a relaxed, unpretentious atmosphere that invites you to linger and enjoy the experience. Our tasting experience was phenomenal.

kaapzicht-wine-estate.author: Tania Muller
kaapzicht-wine-estate.text: Had an amazing time, very relaxed atmosphere. Staff were incredibly welcoming, friendly and helpful. Best service, highly recommend. Great for wine tasting, only pizza and cheese platter for food. Grass and play area for kids

pigcasso.author: Trevor Ayling
pigcasso.text: Fun place for kids. The star pig has sadly passed away but lots of animals to see and feed. Thought R100 was a bit much for food to feed them but otherwise ok. Our granddaughter loved the puppies.

pigcasso.author: Anlé Britz
pigcasso.text: What makes this farm truly special? It’s home to the world’s only painting pigs, where creativity meets nature in the most unexpected and joyful way. While the service and wine were a little mediocre, the overall experience is an absolute must - a one-of-a-kind stop where art, animals, and adventure collide.

pigcasso.author: Devan Pulliah
pigcasso.text: Wow...what an amazing & unique experience...I never expected something like this setup for animals & interaction with humans...its a pet sanctuary but also a lovely cafe, accommodation, wine tasting experience...the manager is very friendly & will to assist...donations are accepted & art painted by the sheep & pig are sold...

phanaprai-thai-massage.author: vongai mukucha
phanaprai-thai-massage.text: Kafee did an amazing massage, one of the best massages I've ever had. I had a buildup of lactic acid, but as soon as I was done, my body was feeling well. I recommend this place, especially if you are into Thai massage. Not forgetting about the lady at the reception, Pinny, she was so friendly and answered all my questions.

phanaprai-thai-massage.author: Ra-eeSA
phanaprai-thai-massage.text: Fantastic service. Staff is respectful and nice. Massage was well worth the price. The space is clean.

phanaprai-thai-massage.author: Herman Stoop
phanaprai-thai-massage.text: My wife and I have been regular clients for some time, and the quality of service has always been exceptional. The team is professional, attentive, and consistently provides a welcoming and relaxing experience. We highly recommend their services and look forward to every visit.

mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.author: Christian Mills
mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.text: Excellent stay at the pool house. The hot tub is great, the braai is nice, and the bedroom has a nice aircon if necessary. Cold continental breakfast was included which was some yoghurt, milk, croissants, fruit bowl, and butter which was great.

mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.author: Leszek Andzel
mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.text: The first words that come to mind are: WOW. I highly recommend booking in advance for the wine tasting, but once you arrive, you’ll experience something truly wonderful — the presentation of wines by the local winemaker, paired with cheese, impressed us with its professionalism.

mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.author: Charlene Blem
mitres-edge-wine-estate-best-boutique-wi.text: Stayed over for one night as we attended a wedding in the area. Unfortunately didn't get a chance to fully explore. Accommodation was comfortable and clean. They served a simple but delicious continental. Absolutely adequate for our needs. They have borehole water which is checked every year.

stellenzicht-wines.author: Nasiphi Matoni
stellenzicht-wines.text: The service was amazing even though it was packed. Our waitress, Yabanathi made sure to keep us in the loop regarding our order, checked in as much as possible without being overwhelming. The scenery was absolutely spectacular, the restrooms were spotless although, again it was packed but soooooo clean.

stellenzicht-wines.author: Alicia Coertze
stellenzicht-wines.text: Wow what an amazing place. Super scenic and so beautiful. Their gluten free pizza was of the best I have had. 10/10 recommend.

stellenzicht-wines.author: Daniel Seha
stellenzicht-wines.text: Sit outside and enjoy good wine, great views and good food. We had the pizzas and they were yummy. A great date spot.

babylonstoren-garden-spa.author: The Wine Girl Cape Town
babylonstoren-garden-spa.text: The garden spa really makes you feel like you are in the garden, surrounded by nature, fresh air and the mountains. There is quite a walk to get here though from the parking area so be prepared to walk about 1km. The massage itself was professional and up to standard.

babylonstoren-garden-spa.author: Danelle de Wet
babylonstoren-garden-spa.text: The massage therapists were absolutely incredible and out of this world - and the only highlight of the entire experience. (They get 5 stars from me). Every treatment felt luxurious, relaxing, and delivered with genuine care and professionalism.

babylonstoren-garden-spa.author: Simone Boshoff
babylonstoren-garden-spa.text: We went there for my Birthday and it was amazing like always they gave me a bunch of flowers upon arrival because, it was my birthday ❤️ the people, service, goods, coffee and the atmosphere is nothing like i have ever seen on a wine / restaurant and spa farm they are so down to earth and i love that!

rupert-rothschild-vignerons.author: Stefan B
rupert-rothschild-vignerons.text: venue that blends elegance with warmth, set in a truly stunning location. It’s the kind of place where you immediately feel relaxed and ready to enjoy a long, leisurely afternoon. The food was excellent: well-prepared, flavorful, and served in generous portions. Every course felt thoughtfully put together and beautifully presented.

rupert-rothschild-vignerons.author: John Sterling
rupert-rothschild-vignerons.text: I was really looking forward to this lunch as I have been drinking their wine for years with pleasure. I'm afraid it was a great disappointment. Expecting a more traditional architecture, I found a very modern and unattractive venue. The staff were welcoming and friendly.

rupert-rothschild-vignerons.author: Arnold Petersen
rupert-rothschild-vignerons.text: A truly exceptional dining experience! The tasting menu showcases stunning seasonal dishes, each thoughtfully paired with wines from the estate by knowledgeable sommeliers. The service team strikes the perfect balance between attentiveness and discretion.

stony-brook-vineyards.author: Marta Baracchini
stony-brook-vineyards.text: A real, wonderful and sincerely great tasting experience at this winery that you should definitely visit asap. Wine quality is top notch and everting else is just perfect. The lady introducing the wines understood our taste and was extremely helpful and made our time there so special. Thanks for the good times. Cheers from Italy.

stony-brook-vineyards.author: Mark Hall
stony-brook-vineyards.text: We had a great tasting at Stony Brook. Great tasting wine, big reds with depth , very knowledgeable staff with loads of information about the wine and farm. Beautiful views. Off the beaten path. Highly recommend.

stony-brook-vineyards.author: Jen Fox
stony-brook-vineyards.text: Dropped in for a tasting in March and enjoyed all the wines we tried. Very calm peaceful setting and everyone was so friendly. The Lyle MCC was our favourite and the rose was also wonderful. Also enjoyed the vineyard dog (feel awful I forgot her name!)

glenelly-estate.author: Albert
glenelly-estate.text: Visited the glass museum and had booked lunch on the terrace outside. The museum has a very wide ranging collection. Top pieces and beautifully presented. The bistro has a stunning setting. The whole place is understated elegance, seats are comfortable and the menu provides ample choices.

glenelly-estate.author: Susanne McInnes
glenelly-estate.text: Our visit to Glenelly was absolutely fabulous. The setting is breathtaking, the cellar omg and the arrangement in the glass museum is truly beautiful. The wine tasting with Lomay was very informative and delivered with such passion. All of the wines were incredible in their own way, and the Lady May left us quite lost for words!

glenelly-estate.author: Sean Kruger
glenelly-estate.text: Glenelly Estate in Stellenbosch delivers a notably high quality tasting experience. The property is meticulously maintained, with ample parking, refined facilities, and expansive views over the Simonsberg slopes that set a confident and composed tone. The wines stand out for their precision, depth, and consistency across the range.

ristorante-pappa-grappa.author: Dewald Reinke
ristorante-pappa-grappa.text: I always enjoy a visit to Ristorante Pappa Grappa, and this time, visiting for lunch, was no different. As this is a German Fusion restaurant they have a wide selection of german inspired dishes, but have other tasty options as well. We enjoyed the food and drinks with a special stand-out being their garlic snails.

ristorante-pappa-grappa.author: Anais C. Maritz
ristorante-pappa-grappa.text: We had a lovely afternoon experience. The food and the atmosphere is exactly what I needed, outdoors and peaceful.

ristorante-pappa-grappa.author: Michelle H
ristorante-pappa-grappa.text: Always the best & friendly service from all staff! For the perfect lunch, pop in and enjoy a delicious crisp wood fire pizza.

kleinood.author: Катарина Кохович
kleinood.text: I always try to choose small, family-owned wineries and this time I definitely didn’t miss. I’ve been to many places, including the well-known ones with tons of reviews, but somehow my heart led me here. This is truly one of the most beautiful experiences , everything feels so genuine and soulful.

kleinood.author: Beaudine Guse
kleinood.text: The real deal! As locals in Stellenbosch we can’t believe it took us this long to visit Kleinood. The wines are exceptional, you can really taste the artistry and thought behind them. The tasting platter was beautifully curated and the ceramicware was absolutely stunning (I wish it was for sale!).

kleinood.author: Nicole Noppe
kleinood.text: We had the most magical experience! We went for the wine tasting with a custom vegetarian seasonal platter for a birthday celebration. From the intimate setting to the service, food and wine, Kleinood was the highlight of our trip. Thank you to the team for treating us so well.

vend-me-wine-estate.author: Morgan Stefanie
vend-me-wine-estate.text: My and my parents had the most wonderful stay at the Vendome guest lodge this past weekend. The rooms were so charming and neat, the hostess was lovely & the breakfast was out of this world! We really enjoyed our stay there. Vendome is a hidden gem in Paarl that’s worth visiting!

vend-me-wine-estate.author: Samantha Robler
vend-me-wine-estate.text: I stayed here at this multi-generation family-owned estate one night following a two-week work trip. I wish I had stayed longer. The staff were incredibly kind and helpful. The grounds were immaculate and tranquil. The rooms were lovely and the bed very very comfortable.

vend-me-wine-estate.author: Evelyn Amoah-Bertrand
vend-me-wine-estate.text: Stunning place ! My sister and I really enjoyed our stay 🩷 Lorna our host certainly made our stay 🩷💗

black-elephant-vintners.author: Debbie Visser
black-elephant-vintners.text: Such a unique experience. The wines were amazing and the story behind the whole operation is amazing. Dreams do come true. The music with the tasting is si unique, absolutely loved it!

black-elephant-vintners.author: Karo HP
black-elephant-vintners.text: Black Elephant Vintners was one of the wineries we chose to visit with our international friends because we wanted to show them something a little different from the typical Franschhoek wine experience. It turned out to be one of the highlights of our trip. This is not your traditional wine tasting.

black-elephant-vintners.author: Jan
black-elephant-vintners.text: We stopped by spontaneously in the afternoon around 4 p.m. and were looked after by Rowan. He was extremely friendly, welcoming, and attentive, and explained the wines to us in an excellent and knowledgeable way. The wines were all very delicious and of great quality.

flagstone-wines.author: Howie Warner
flagstone-wines.text: The limited edition lunches served up by Mark at month end are absolutely superb and even better when paired with the fabulous Flagstone wines

flagstone-wines.author: Gerhard
flagstone-wines.text: It’s a relatively small and intimate wine cellar where you can drink, taste and buy bottles of wine. There is a restaurant as well. It’s not a wine farm with vineyards. They have an excellent selection of wine, I really enjoyed their Pinotage and Shiraz.

flagstone-wines.author: Christine Syme
flagstone-wines.text: We discovered Flagstone at a dinner party where one of their guests who bought wines for clients in the U.K., raved about Flagstone. We popped in to taste - 11 years ago, we still wine taste, we told all our friends about their superb wines.

klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.author: Fezeka
klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.text: I visited Klein Alto yesterday and had a wonderful time with friends. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, and the food was amazing. A special thank you to Likho, our waiter, for his excellent recommendations and attentive service.

klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.author: Astrid Witteveen
klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.text: After 3 visits the last year I can honestly review Klein Alto. It’s mainly about the location, especially the deck is nice for a relaxt Sunday lunch. The food is just average, not bad but for this price point you can get better quality at upmarket restaurants in the area.

klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.author: Kael
klein-alto-wine-tasting-restaurant.text: Google reviews have two key features. Firstly, they’re time-limited, and secondly, they’re only as good as the person leaving the review. I’ve never been to Alto before. It hasn’t been open for very long, though.

natte-valleij.author: Wade Solomon
natte-valleij.text: We attended a wedding here and it was a lovely place to celebrate the marriage of our friends. They've got an awesome setup with beautiful trees and a little mini-golf area too. The facilities were really well set out.

natte-valleij.author: amos mwase
natte-valleij.text: The place looks great! What you see outside is just its natural beauty, but inside the rooms are elegant. I love it it’s nice and quiet Outside just need tired up but love the place

natte-valleij.author: Havashini Pillay
natte-valleij.text: Heads up to future bridal couples. Natte Valleij has beautiful gardens, but our wedding experience in April was disappointing in many respects. The cellar reception area was meant to be repainted before wedding season (including ours), but that never happened.

leo-sky-farm.author: Emelia Conroy
leo-sky-farm.text: Leo Sky Farm is honestly one of the most beautiful and peaceful places I have ever visited. From the moment you arrive, you can feel how much love and care has gone into every little detail. The accommodation is absolutely stunning, clean, beautifully decorated, and so comfortable.

leo-sky-farm.author: Monique Schmidt
leo-sky-farm.text: Absolutely loved our stay at Leo Sky Farm.The cottages are beautifully designed and incredibly comfortable, making it the perfect escape. The hot tubs were such a highlight, relaxing with those breathtaking views was just unforgettable.

leo-sky-farm.author: Eliska Vermeulen
leo-sky-farm.text: Absolutely loved our stay at Leo Sky Farm. From the moment we arrived, everything felt thoughtfully curated and effortlessly relaxing. The cottages are beautifully designed, stylish yet cosy, and equipped with everything you need for a comfortable escape.

boekenhoutskloof-winery.author: Jonathan Nugent
boekenhoutskloof-winery.text: I’m very late posting this review having visited in 2017(!) - but want to do so as it was truly an outstanding experience at a top quality producer. Felt like a valued guest rather than a tourist, and the experience was so personal and informative. The wine was amazing.

boekenhoutskloof-winery.author: Liesl Jobson
boekenhoutskloof-winery.text: Another fabulous purchase at special rates after attending the local book festival. The grounds are so beautiful and it’s a tonic to walk down to the vineyards below the tasting area. Rich history and impressive service from every staff member. Very slick process of tasting and ordering, paying and packing.

boekenhoutskloof-winery.author: Sleepy Eddie James
boekenhoutskloof-winery.text: This is one of the best wineries in all of South Africa. The tour and chat with Gottfried the wine maker is especially exciting for knowledgeable enophiles. Otherwise, buckle up and enjoy the vibe and generous tasting offered afterwards.

krismiskamp.author: Natalie Van Dijk
krismiskamp.text: Loved this picnic spot! Calm and neat, perfect to enjoy with friends and family. Half of us hiked up and met the others at the top. There is a long drop loo, but the toilet paper ran out in the afternoon, so pack some in case. Lots of trees with shade and braai spots 🌳🧺🍗 Get there early to secure a spot.

krismiskamp.author: Rob Yates
krismiskamp.text: Little gem of a Camp site in the confines of Paarl Nature reserve where you can build up an appetite by talking a good solid hike to either Paarl, Brianje or Gordon's rock, where you have amazing views for days.

krismiskamp.author: Peter Erasmus
krismiskamp.text: An ideal spot for a picnic in the week. The area was spotless. Remember to bring your own water as there is none available on site. The solitude that can be found here is food for the soul.

lievland-vineyards.author: Myles Williams
lievland-vineyards.text: Visited Lievland Winery and had a really lovely experience. 🍷 The tasting included six wines, all different enough to keep things interesting. The white wines were fresh and easy to enjoy.

lievland-vineyards.author: J B M
lievland-vineyards.text: We came accross Lievland by accident and what an amazing accident- we had the best time on a beautiful Sunday afternoon sitting on the terrace drinking and eating - I am sitting at home in wet England drinking the best Cinsault wine and wishing it was last Sunday. We will definitely be back and next time will have the Sunday Roast.

lievland-vineyards.author: Debby Bornmann
lievland-vineyards.text: Lievland is a beautiful little gem with a good breakfast and a lovely wine- chocolate tasting. It’s not that busy so we enjoyed our tranquility there. Would love to come again.

groenland-wyne.author: Peter Chong
groenland-wyne.text: Highly recommended worth a visit especially for the wine connoisseur! Both white and red ranges stood out with bursting bouquet of fragrance, fruitiness, body and individual character. Staff knowledge of wine from history, plantation and maturation is impeccable. Prices are very cheap or more affordable compared to bigger wineries.

groenland-wyne.author: Angie Gibson
groenland-wyne.text: Excellent- Susanna gave us a great explanations on the wines. The wines were all to our taste and we bought a few bottles!

groenland-wyne.author: Anneke Myburgh
groenland-wyne.text: What lovely afternoon spend with hospitality from everyone including the dogs. And the wine is so good! We left with 16 and it is so reasonable

mont-marie-wine-estate.author: Kevin Seeberger
mont-marie-wine-estate.text: Just amazing! The view, the service, the food the wine. Warm welcoming stuff, superb service and excellent food and wine for a more than reasonable price. Highly recommend! We will definitely come back if we have the chance.

mont-marie-wine-estate.author: Jacobus Mostert
mont-marie-wine-estate.text: I have been to a lot of wine tastings, but this has by far been my worst experience ever. The waiter was absolutely incompetent and didn't know the basics of wines. Stated that the tannins on the unwooded Chardonnay was "great" - you don't get noticeable tannins on white grape varietals.

mont-marie-wine-estate.author: Raymond Walters
mont-marie-wine-estate.text: I had the best pizza of my entire life. Paired with stunning scenery, this is a must try!

koopmanskloof-winery.author: Werner Dalziel
koopmanskloof-winery.text: Did a hike on the farm. Not to difficult. Stunning views of Table Mountain and surrounds.

koopmanskloof-winery.author: Gerhard Dekker
koopmanskloof-winery.text: Wines are good and priced very reasonable. Staff friendly and helpful. Not on a wine route but excellent wines to buy.

koopmanskloof-winery.author: Melissa Claasen
koopmanskloof-winery.text: Disappointed. You drive far into the farm to get to any buildings, then you have to ask around to find a place to do a tasting. You go inside to find out that they don't do tastings, despite boards outside saying "tasting and sales". Unhelpful staff sadly.

groot-parys-wine-estate.author: Frank Vollering
groot-parys-wine-estate.text: Wine tasting and vineyard tour was an amazing experience! Eric and Mariette are a magnificent couple that have moved to south africa with nothing 25 years ago to start the journey of starting a wine estate. Their wines are superb! Incredible to hear their stories during the tour. Highly recommend!

groot-parys-wine-estate.author: Willie van Zyl
groot-parys-wine-estate.text: Fantastic atmosphere, great food and drinks and a very smart, newly created play area for the kids.

groot-parys-wine-estate.author: Dave Onearth
groot-parys-wine-estate.text: This is going to be one of the most beautiful retirement estates in South Africa.

the-simondium-guild.author: Rabiya Hendricks-Small
