the-simondium-guild.text: A true "hidden gem"! Our car overheated on the road just outside the entrance on our way to celebrate a birthday. We then changed our plans and went inside, and what a lovely experience! Everything you need for a lekker day out with friend and family in one pretty place!

the-simondium-guild.author: Jacques Cronjé
the-simondium-guild.text: I’ve driven past the sign numerous times on my way somewhere else but don’t let the bland street facing facade deter you. Behind it is where the magic lies, the place truly is a destination. Catering for all your tastes, beers and burgers, pizzas and wine, craft gin, brandy and rum. And smoked meats!

the-simondium-guild.author: Ashley Naudé
the-simondium-guild.text: New and unique experience, worth the visit. A hidden gem on the outskirts of Paarl. It offers a real market experience as you can order food and drinks from the various restaurants and sit outside and enjoy something from each store. There is a glass shop which offers painting workshops too.

stellekaya-wines.author: A M
stellekaya-wines.text: Jane , wonderfully assisted by her team, instills passion into all dealings . An amazing tasting session with lots of information on the wines right down to food pairs. The Hercules remains a favourite. Perfect for winter with friends

stellekaya-wines.author: Robert Francis
stellekaya-wines.text: The amount of Passion they put in to their wine's, well it shows. One of my favorites out there. Make a appointment for the wine tasting and see the Passion and honesty..

stellekaya-wines.author: Steve Horvath
stellekaya-wines.text: Stellakaya makes several delicious wine offerings named from the stars in several constellations. Jane, the owner, is a lovely host and can tell you all about the wine.

domaine-coutelier.author: Michael Wassiluk
domaine-coutelier.text: We stayed 3 nights at Domaine Coutelier in the sunrise apartment. I did the booking via sa-venues where you can’t see any valuations. Unfortunately. At Domaine Coutelier there is a huuuuuge maintenance backlog. And it is a kind of messy outside.

domaine-coutelier.author: Jos van der Meer
domaine-coutelier.text: Horrible experience in a neglected domaine. Where to start? The place looked like a construction area. The garden was full of dog poo. The main pool needs maintenance, like everything else. Moonrise cottage: torn bed sheets, some sockets are broken and dangerous, lamps are broken.

domaine-coutelier.author: Giasmin Ferrari
domaine-coutelier.text: Absolutely loved our stay in the Sunset Cottage! The views were breathtaking, and the peaceful surroundings made for the perfect escape from the city. The self check-in and check-out process was seamless and gave us total freedom, which we really appreciated.

mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.author: Helen Broekmann
mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.text: The scenery is lovely but there is a bit of work to be done on the trails- the signs to the various trails are almost illegible and many of the wooden logs originally installed as steps have been burned in forest fires and erosion has taken place where they were so there are metal spikes sticking up that used to hold the logs but are now…

mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.author: Emily Vousden
mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.text: This place was stunning. I do recommend checking weather to work out how chilly it will be and whether the cloud cover with come in. Theres easier short routes but most are medium to hard. Also I went post fire so some paths were closed.

mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.author: Ishmael P Ngosi
mont-rochelle-nature-reserve.text: Such an amazing hike! I started from the bottom near Groendal and climbed up. It took me 2 full hours to get to the top, but it was totally worth the effort. The big rocks and caves along the way are really cool great for taking photos.

helderberg-nature-reserve.author: IZYCRAFTS
helderberg-nature-reserve.text: Loved the trail we chose. Would definitely do it again and choose a different path. It was sad about the hiker found close to the Dome that is why its soo important to not hike alone. May his soul RIP.. Altho I confess to hiking alone in the Reserves because its a peaceful destress experience for alone time.

helderberg-nature-reserve.author: Rafique Begg
helderberg-nature-reserve.text: What a great hike. Just under 3 hours and not doing the peaks yet. The view of False Bay and surrounds are stunning. Multiple trails to choose from and exits if you feel too exhausted. Highly recommended.

helderberg-nature-reserve.author: Nico van der Walt
helderberg-nature-reserve.text: An absolutely beautiful nature reserve, well looked after. Some food coffee to regain energy after a hike. Many trails to walk, ranging from your beginners to more advanced hikers 🥰 definitely recommend Helderberg Nature reserve. Beautiful views the higher you go

radloff-park-dog-park.author: Trystan William Gale
radloff-park-dog-park.text: This dog park is off leash, there are ample poop bags situated throughout. On hot days, the river works as a lekker cool down for your furry friend. Lekker open, some well placed trees for shade. As always odd person who does not pick up poop, or let's their, uhmm, boisterous dog of leash.

radloff-park-dog-park.author: Faye
radloff-park-dog-park.text: Beautiful dog park. Thank you to all who maintain it. It’s such a wonderful place for dogs and their people to get exercise or relax. Only a shame that not everyone uses the provided (free) waste bags and bins.

radloff-park-dog-park.author: Hayly Page
radloff-park-dog-park.text: Great Park for off leash walls and my doggies enjoy splashing in the river. Facilities are nice and clean.

eden-forest.author: MarnoCrafford
eden-forest.text: Absolutely beautiful hike! We did the Stellenbosch Mountain summit via Paradyskloof / Eden Forest and the scenery was incredible the whole way up. The route is well worth it, but it is definitely a very strenuous hike with a steep climb in sections.

eden-forest.author: Saki Marais
eden-forest.text: Beautiful hiking spot! Definitely worth a look!

eden-forest.author: Miroda Otto
eden-forest.text: One of my favourite running and walking spots in Stellenbosch. Lots of people walking their dogs as well

helderberg-marine-protected-area.author: Viola Inches
helderberg-marine-protected-area.text: Equisite Area to conserve Birds habitat area. I walk daily. If we urgently make an effect to clean and not use methods to entervere with mother nature fury babies. Much appreciated Viola Inches Member of The Lionsclub Gordonsbay South africa.

helderberg-marine-protected-area.author: Bridget.
helderberg-marine-protected-area.text: This beach is very beautiful and quiet during weekdays but please don't be alone.

helderberg-marine-protected-area.author: Malicia Lott
helderberg-marine-protected-area.text: Macassar Beach Great fishing spot, but do not go alone.

new-orleans-park.author: Bevan Oliver
new-orleans-park.text: Its a nice open area it gives you a nature vibe very relaxing you can braai some of the area's like the chairs needs DIY but futher on its a nice place to go to with friends and family for a relaxing time

new-orleans-park.author: Abubaker Francis
new-orleans-park.text: Nice vibe,good braai spots and green lawns,enjoyed it there on heritage day.

new-orleans-park.author: Megan Swanepoel
new-orleans-park.text: Amazing place for family events , birthday and the list goes on .

jan-marais-park.author: Benjamin Griffin
jan-marais-park.text: Increadible small park for walks and stolls. Has a nice botanical esk gardens at the start with more natural vegetation in the rest of the park. Safe and some cool wildlife if you look hard enough. I have spent many happy hours here.

jan-marais-park.author: Adeola Omotosho
jan-marais-park.text: A very spiritual Natural Reserve that has artworks from 1966 and play grounds and mechanical fitness area.

jan-marais-park.author: Zeane Nel
jan-marais-park.text: A lovely spot to picnic, run or excercise. The indigenous gardens are breathtakingly beautiful and a must-see when visiting Stellies. Although there are cameras in some areas, I would not recommend that women visit the park on their own at late hours.

bretagne-rock.author: Ryan Abram
bretagne-rock.text: A very scenic spot in Paarl. You can drive up to a sort of parking area (limited to I would say 4-5 cars max) and then walk on the wooden deck trail. Bretagne Rock is actually very close to the parking, but it is a fairly steep climb up. The views are worth it once you get to the top, panoramic and picturesque.

bretagne-rock.author: Nur
bretagne-rock.text: This trail offers a stunning hiking experience just outside Cape Town. The trail is well-marked and moderately challenging, making it suitable for various fitness levels. The panoramic views from the top are breathtaking, showcasing the surrounding vineyards and mountains.

bretagne-rock.author: Dylan Botha
bretagne-rock.text: Such an incredible gem! Lots of camp sites to stop and picnic or braai and lots of places to do abseiling(with your own gear) Breathtaking Views: The panoramic view of the lakes and mountains are unbelievable.

limietberg-nature-reserve.author: Rock Adventures
limietberg-nature-reserve.text: The Rockhopper is an amazing trail. You are going to get wet! Pristine pools and a rocky freehike. You need reasonable fitness and take care of your ankles. We drank the water with no issues. Please take your waste back out with you so that we can keep the trail in pristine condition.

limietberg-nature-reserve.author: Ladyironhide
limietberg-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful place, with amazing views but its not an easy hike like everyone said it was. Its not for first timers to go through alone, believe me, i did it cause my sister ditched me and almost got lost and almost fell. Luckily i met some lovely hikers that helped me through a panic attack and helped me get to the top.

limietberg-nature-reserve.author: Roekayda Adams-Kupido
limietberg-nature-reserve.text: After our concerns about our safety during our stay, we got some positive feedback; stating.... "We acknowledge the inconvenience of not being able to fully enjoy your stay at Twee de Tol. Your concerns and overall experience, as raised in your correspondence, are noted and appreciated.

coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.author: Ahesha Adams (AJ)
coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.text: We walked our own trail because the trails are only marked on the map, nowhere else on the ground. Boyfriend was not happy that we didn't know if we're hiking the correct path or not. Beautiful view and safe place

coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.author: Nina Naude
coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.text: For all the fitness junkies, runners, cyclists as well as nature lovers. Safe and beautiful trails against the mountain with stunning views. Whether you hike up to the 2nd cross or only stroll along the contour paths it is definitely worth it. #coetzenburgtrails

coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.author: Līvija Strazdiņa
coetzenburg-mountain-bike-trails.text: great place for a walk! i enjoyed every moment there! Beautiful place!

paarl-arboretum.author: Sandra Fraefel
paarl-arboretum.text: We love this park to walk our dogs. Early in the morning it's not very crowded. Don't go on Saturday morning, there is a park run and it gets very busy. Other than that it's fine. One loop is about 5km and the scenery changes from the entrance to the back of the park. Clean toilets and lots of parking.

paarl-arboretum.author: Xena-Marie Horn
paarl-arboretum.text: Stunning park, very well maintained, clean and free to explore! Sat on the opposite side of the river from town, we walked from the train station end in and over the bridge and then down the main high street! It’s a very peaceful place to relax!

paarl-arboretum.author: Heinrich Hendricks
paarl-arboretum.text: Had good time here, very good and friendly people, nice place to walk and run, also you can take a dive if you want, it's family friendly and relaxing.

hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.author: Catherine Kühn
hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.text: A beautiful hike with picture perfect views in the most amazing setting. Steep hiking and a good level of fitness is required. The nice part about this route is you can do the circular route and therefore not go back the same way down. The trails are well kept and well marked.

hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.author: Bruce Moore
hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.text: It's a fantastic hike. 14km and 1000m elevation gain circular route. You do need to be fit to do this hike. Did it in winter in nice weather. I think I'd avoid the heat in summer. We did the circuit anticlockwise to avoid the steep climb out at the end. There is no shade or shelter on the route.

hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.author: Donovan Weber
hiking-perdekop-hiking-trail-mont-rochel.text: Awesome hike with breathtaking views, take lots of water and push through. It’s a 13-14km hike with 1000m altitude gain. Yes you need to have a higher than average fitness level or a strong level of endurance and confidence.

strand-golf-club.author: David Martin
strand-golf-club.text: Excellent Service from the young men in the Pro shop on Monday and Tuesday afternoon.Such good Service,with a friendly greeting and how may we be of assistance.One feels welcome straight away.The course is in fantastic condition.I know that Strand has water shortages,when we get back from the Garden route I will definitely be back to play…

strand-golf-club.author: SA CCJ
strand-golf-club.text: Strand Golf Club is a scenic 18-hole course located near the coastline in the Western Cape, offering a relaxed yet strategic playing experience. The front nine is relatively narrow with softer fairway turf, requiring accurate tee shots and smart course management.

strand-golf-club.author: Kai Feuerstake
strand-golf-club.text: Strand Golf Club – A Windy Challenge with Room for Improvement Strand Golf Club is beautifully located between False Bay and the Hottentots-Holland mountains, promising a scenic golfing experience—if it weren’t for the wind. Extra balls are highly recommended, as the gusts can make for a tricky round.

stellenbosch-golf-club.author: Dennis Hines
stellenbosch-golf-club.text: Great golf course halfway hse ladies were amazing & helpful. Guest's enjoyed their game of golf & the pizzas 🍕 afterwards with a good couple glasses of wine.🍷🍷😃 Kind regards Dennis- Funtastique Shuttles & Tours

stellenbosch-golf-club.author: YourGP Golf
stellenbosch-golf-club.text: AVOID at all costs. As a guest, you don’t feel welcome from the front door. Services are low level, or don’t exist at all - useless “desk” at the front door; - disorganized proshop; - No-starter = disorganized tee times; - caddie master??where?! - no StrokeSaver book available but tons of hidden obstacles in play!

stellenbosch-golf-club.author: Rudi Burger
stellenbosch-golf-club.text: Well if i did not experience it by myself i would not belief it. It us absolutely amazing the setup and great golfing vibe.

hostile-and-munition-paintball.author: Shireen Price
hostile-and-munition-paintball.text: We recently hosted my daughter’s birthday at Munition Paintball and had such a great experience. The kids absolutely loved it and were fully entertained from start to finish. A special mention to Aiden, the marshal, who was incredibly friendly, hands-on, and made sure everything ran smoothly while keeping the kids safe and engaged.

hostile-and-munition-paintball.author: poohcii cpg
hostile-and-munition-paintball.text: Good morning Alex My brothers and i cant thank you enough for accommodating us this weekend, the boys cant stop the praise for the service and help we got from yourself and Aiden. Solid solid weekend really, my brother was so surprised for it all, Mission Accomplished. Definitely won't be the last time my brother. Thank you once again

hostile-and-munition-paintball.author: Tristan Coetzee
hostile-and-munition-paintball.text: GREAT experience as always. Alex as his crew go out their way to make sure you have a great time! I've hosted a couple of events and they went the extra mile for team building. David, is a great instructor.

reapers-paintball.author: Alecia Labuschagne
reapers-paintball.text: What a great day for kids and adults. We had sooooo much fun. Great for birthday parties, team building or even just for a girl outing to release build up stress ... Can not wait to go back again

reapers-paintball.author: Myfanwy Abernethy
reapers-paintball.text: Absolutely awesome experience! My son's 12th Birthday Party was "the best birthday ever" thanks to Alan, Jana-Marie and the team of marshals. Very professional in a relaxed, fun, adventure-filled setting. Definitely recommend!

reapers-paintball.author: Craig Peters
reapers-paintball.text: Good fun we had at Reapers. It hurts a little but I had a good time. Allen the owner is a real cool guy and made sure we all played by the rules. Can't wait for the next game. Bring it on.

somerset-west-golf-club.author: Sahar Antoine
somerset-west-golf-club.text: Nice golf but not extraordinary. Greenfees are very expensive (r530) for a 9 holes. You can play better for cheaper around : Strand. They refused affiliated rate to many member fo other golf course. Unacceptable

somerset-west-golf-club.author: Beverley Walker (BW Aerial View)
somerset-west-golf-club.text: The course is in great shape and the clubhouse is awesome. Very friendly staff in the restaurant.

somerset-west-golf-club.author: Adwill Alexander
somerset-west-golf-club.text: Rock solid NPO hosted their first Golf day fundraiser at SWGC and it was great....we had over 100 golfers who signed up and played today, the weather great and the atmosphere was awesome!!! Food, drinks and service was top class...

the-golf-village-somerset-west.author: Hardus Steenkamp
the-golf-village-somerset-west.text: Lovely experience at the golf village. Very upmarket venue with really affordable pricing. The scenery and setting is absolutely stunning. Very impressed. Together with De Kelders they can make a huge success.

the-golf-village-somerset-west.author: Pieter van der Lith
the-golf-village-somerset-west.text: Great experience. People were stunning and very helpful. Area is well looked after and the amenities are excellent. Awesome experience, will definitely visit them again.

the-golf-village-somerset-west.author: Stuart Dickonson
the-golf-village-somerset-west.text: I do not want to free visit. The right thing to have done is do a refund. It does not matter to me, because I will not go back. Dear Charnelle I want to emphasize that I am not to be confused with any other individual who may have previously raised complaints about your facility. My experience was unique to my visit.

g-spot-mtb-trails.author: Melinda Dolack
g-spot-mtb-trails.text: Excellent trails for all skill levels. Rolling easy flow to jumps and technical riding...love the motivational signs...

g-spot-mtb-trails.author: Abdul Ismail
g-spot-mtb-trails.text: A nice trail - will definitely make a turn again to experience more thereof. More signage than at Eden forest.

g-spot-mtb-trails.author: The Amped Life with Chris
g-spot-mtb-trails.text: Fantastic place for MTB or eMTB riding for all skill levels. Also the gateway to many other epic trails in the area. A MUST for riders visiting Stellenbosch.

welgelegen-farm.author: Tobias Mostert
welgelegen-farm.text: Awesome route with nice challenges and some sissy escape routes for the faint of heart. Breathtaking views and really nice braai spots available.. Booking essential. Flushing toilet and basin with clean water available . They even offer camping with tents and braai packs on request. God willing we will be back for some more off-road fun!

welgelegen-farm.author: Johan Alberts
welgelegen-farm.text: Scenic 4 x 4 trail, at times nail-biting, but a fantastic experience! Beautiful braai-areas - bring your family and friends and enjoy an unforgetable day in nature. PS: as always, when you take on any off-road trail, be fully prepared (both vehicle and driver). Advice: travel in a [2-vehicle] convoy.

welgelegen-farm.author: Francois Muntingh
welgelegen-farm.text: What a great place!!! Best views ever, and trails allow for novice to experienced drivers with entry level or full extreme set-ups.

the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.author: Simon Shave
the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.text: Skatepark undergoing some maintenance at the time of visit. Bowl was out of order. Elsewhere some of the concrete was flaking in places. Plenty of shade, seating and refreshments.

the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.author: Nikie De Villiers
the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.text: Fun park and safe with a good vibe. Designed more for Inline skates or MBX but still good if you have some skill. Steep transition on the big ramps. Very fun bowl. The coping in the park is quite high, that makes it tricky for skateboarding and especially hard for beginners.

the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.author: Roy Doust
the-i2b-somerset-west-skatepark.text: Neat tidy park, well kept and plenty of every thing. Neat wooden bowel. Glad to see they have painted the ramps.

green-acres-riding-school.author: Anne-Marie Engelbrecht
green-acres-riding-school.text: A love for horses, a passion with children and open space! Out rides, techniques, western mounted games and so so much more...... this is the place to be

green-acres-riding-school.author: Silver Cat
green-acres-riding-school.text: A wonderfully relaxed atmosphere on a farm. The owner of the riding school is also the instructor. Classes are kept nice and small so that each student receives personal attention. The horses are very well looked after.

green-acres-riding-school.author: Erna van Vuuren
green-acres-riding-school.text: We went as a big group, many first time riders for an outride. The guides and horses was amazing. Not to mention the scenery. A true breath of fresh air from town! You need to do this!! Awesome place

de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.author: Givemore Mabena
de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.text: It's a very beautiful estate located near the strawberry farms

de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.author: Bekezela qhue
de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.text: Golf course is big, well maintained, The place is so large you can do enough cycling or jogging within boundary Without exiting the gate. Big leafy trees surrounding the place it felt like we were taking a "nature walk" seeing creatures like Tortoise and birds. Warm welcome.

de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.author: Suzette Newlands
de-zalze-winelands-golf-estate.text: Service was excellent accommodation very good. Breathtaking views. Will visit again.

maties-rock-climbing-wall.author: Anker Gouws
maties-rock-climbing-wall.text: Great atmosphere and excellent new climbing holds. Here is where you meet fun and adventurous people in Stellenbosch. Join the club for climbing expeditions. 5 stars

maties-rock-climbing-wall.author: Mr T
maties-rock-climbing-wall.text: This wall is great for those that can already climb. Definitely not beginner friendly for those that want to start bouldering - not sure why there is no flat wall for the beginners to start off as not everyone is able to do overhangs straight off the bat. This can be quite discouraging for those new to bouldering and starting out.

maties-rock-climbing-wall.author: John Terblanche
maties-rock-climbing-wall.text: things are greener on the other side of the boerewors curtain... This is by far the best indoor climbing wall in the Stellenbosch winelands area, and a place for anyone keen on training to pursue the sport. it also allows one to meet up with like-minded folk and get access to outdoor climbing group! good vibes and happy training!

mini-ninja-winelands.author: Nazia B
mini-ninja-winelands.text: The manager Usama was such a great host - so accommodating with our needs and answered all our questions thoroughly. His an attentive manager and knows how to please his patrons. We had enquired about the food being halaal and he was honest and polite. Staff were attentive with my kids. Will definitely be visiting again

mini-ninja-winelands.author: Robyn Hueber
mini-ninja-winelands.text: This is the best play park for young kids I have ever seen. Everything is so well organised and thought out. My son had such a great time. The staff are amazing with kids and know how to entertain them and be their ‘best friend’ so you can relax or get some work done. The play equipment is so much better than anything I have seen.

mini-ninja-winelands.author: Gisela Patlansky
mini-ninja-winelands.text: This is an incredible outing for parents with small kids. The environment is so fun for little kids without being overwhelming. There is great coffee and snacks and the layout allows for spaces for parents to sit and keep an eye on the little ones, while being able to relax themselves. The party rooms are awesome for party groups.

boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.author: jacques Maree
boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.text: Two months later and I still they have not removed me from blocked list even after paying. They will let you in and say they removed you but never happens.

boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.author: Grant Vernon
boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.text: Great venue. Good food, good atmosphere. Great golf course and facilities.

boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.author: Limon Nyoka
boschenmeer-golf-country-estate.text: Quite and great place to relax and freshen up. Did not get a chance to tee a couple of golf balls. Will definetly book a space for golfing because of the quite and peaceful atmosphere.

dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.author: Zama Mlaba
dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.text: An absolutely beautiful and serene experience. The Dylan Lewis Sculpture Garden is a perfect blend of art and nature, with powerful sculptures thoughtfully placed throughout stunning indigenous landscapes. Peaceful, inspiring, and impeccably curated - a must-visit when you're in the Stellenbosch area ✨️

dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.author: georgiev alexander
dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.text: The best sculpture garden in the world. Really. Every detail was well planned, every grass, every flower is at the right place to present the sculptures in the best way. Great experience to walk around surrounded by this strange combination between humans' and God's creativity. Thanks to Mr.Lews and his people for that beauty.

dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.author: Skye Leask
dylan-lewis-studio-sculpture-garden.text: No amount of stars can correctly express what an incredible experience and place this is. Art meets poetry, wilderness and psychology, a profound celebration of the pain and magic of transmutation and metamorphosis.

huguenot-memorial-museum.author: Martin Stevens
huguenot-memorial-museum.text: The Huguenot Memorial Museum in Franschhoek is a world-class museum that offers an engaging and educational experience for visitors of all ages. The exhibits are clearly presented and thoughtfully curated, making the history of the French Huguenots easy to understand and interesting, even for children.

huguenot-memorial-museum.author: Lynn E
huguenot-memorial-museum.text: The displays in the museum are quite explanatory and well-designed. Our tour leader hired a guide for us who was perhaps too informative and we were standing and not walking much for a long time. I broke away to find a bench. It was inconvenient that the only apparent rest room was across the street.

huguenot-memorial-museum.author: Tandyn Gelderman
huguenot-memorial-museum.text: The Huguenot Museum is a beautiful and well-maintained site with lovely gardens and clean, welcoming spaces. The exhibits are fascinating, and I really appreciated the modern touch of wall prints with easy-to-read information.

stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.author: Juandri Supra
stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.text: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Such a beautiful, peaceful spot! ​We had a great time exploring the Stellenbosch Botanical Garden. The walkways and paths wind beautifully through the different sections, making the whole walk feel super relaxed but still interesting.

stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.author: Nyree
stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.text: Beautiful gardens. Lots of little nooks to sit quietly. Lovely gift shop. Relaxing atmosphere.

stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.author: Mrs Moon
stellenbosch-university-botanical-garden.text: Absolutely beautiful gardens. Peaceful and serene. There was a market throughout the garden. R15 entry fee. Lovely coffee shop.

the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.author: Bondura
the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.text: Absolutely blown away by the stunning Alice in Wonderland production! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ The entire show was an excellent delivery from start to finish — creative, polished, and full of energy. The kids were absolutely phenomenal, performing with confidence, enthusiasm, and so much personality. It was incredible to see their hard work shine on stage.

the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.author: Estelle Hester
the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.text: I attended the play, ABSURD PERSON SINGULAR, at the playhouse. We enjoyed having a drink before the start of the play. People were lighthearted in this spacious foyer, staff friendly and helpful.

the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.author: Gerhard
the-playhouse-theatre-somerset-west.text: I have to say I underestimated this theatre. I had a blast with a group of friends. We had wine and watched a show. The show was cleverly written, casting and actors were great, overall production value was high. Thanks to the Playhouse theatre for a fun night out!

rupert-museum.author: Pauline van de venter
rupert-museum.text: Highly recommended!! It was a wonderful experience in a serene setting. The installations were exceptional and the Pierneef panels a highlight ♡ Entrance is free, which was a surprise. We had breakfast in the restaurant, the food was excellent, had a wonderful time. Thanks to the Ruperts for giving so much to the community ♡♡♡

rupert-museum.author: Survivor
rupert-museum.text: Wonderful architecture and quiet setting away from other establishments. The front desk attendant was kind and informative. The place itself is lovely and had a beautiful display of several bonsais, a café, and they facilitate workshops.

rupert-museum.author: Kabelo Mohlabe
rupert-museum.text: It's a nice place to visit, provided you're already in Stellenbosch. The museum is free and open to the public, which compensates for the few exhibitions present.

oude-libertas.author: Nadine Carroll
oude-libertas.text: An absolute must for culture lovers! What a beautiful venue with incredible shows. The 40 Fingers 2 show was such a delight. Highly recommend this wonderful experience beneath the stars. Suited for all ages, although little ones may not last through late night performances ✨👌🏻🎹🎶

oude-libertas.author: jose romeu de abreu
oude-libertas.text: Beautiful Place to be. Green vineyards surround you. Jose. Entrepreneur, Garagiste Winemaker, South African Tour Guide & Photography.

oude-libertas.author: Hanno KK Calitz
oude-libertas.text: Wow. What a fabulous environment to experience the world renowned Indluvo Youth Choir for a sunset concert.

glen-carlou.author: Nolan smith
glen-carlou.text: A real hidden gem. Stunning views. Exceptional service from our waiter. Excellent food and presentation. Enjoyed the salmon starter so much I had to have 2.

glen-carlou.author: Marius Goosen
glen-carlou.text: What a wonderful experience, its a must do. Our host, Shediel, was the best. You have to do this wine tasting to appreciate the science behind wine manufacturing. Dhe explained everything in detail. They also do the bottling and labeling and distribution. Do this thing.

glen-carlou.author: Alison Graney
glen-carlou.text: We visited Glen Carlou Wine Estate's restaurant and wine tasting facility. Their food was exceptional as was the service from Muriel. We started off with a Chef's treat which was potatoe and bacon soup. Starters My husband had as a pulled beef and onion soup paired with Glen Carlou Grand Classique.

drostdy-theatre.author: Simone Tyers
drostdy-theatre.text: The show was amazing. The theatre sound and lighting was great and the little coffee shop session before the show was lovely. Busy but served quickly. The loo facility was clean and fresh despite the amount of people.

drostdy-theatre.author: Luna Paige
drostdy-theatre.text: A gem in Stellenbosch. Stylish, well-kept theatre with great facilities. A little escape from reality. Balcony lounge for smokers and non-smokers, an inside bar in comfortable space to sit and socialise. Safe and secure underground parking.

drostdy-theatre.author: Hermann Schultz
drostdy-theatre.text: Went to see Nataniel here. Stunning Venue, efficient bar, nice cozy theatre I am probably biased as there are many if my wifes oil paintings in the walls 😃

the-drama-factory.author: André Boshoff
the-drama-factory.text: Great new venue for shows. We.loved being there. Simple startup with everything you need. Very sociable. GO THERE!! Let's get arts and culture culture going again in South Africa as per pre-1994

the-drama-factory.author: Sonja Fitzell
the-drama-factory.text: Absolutely fantastic. The best part is that it is on our doorstep. Fabulous.

the-drama-factory.author: Atomic-Chicken
the-drama-factory.text: Small little theatre with a stunning personality 😀 I ❤️ the whole vibe of the place! The ladies at reception were super friendly and quick with our orders. They offer some snacks 🍫🍬🍭, coffee ☕, hot chocolate and WINE 🍷 These are what I recall seeing, there were more options available on the menu.

the-gallery-at-grande-provence.author: Nicole Gething
the-gallery-at-grande-provence.text: This establishment offers a truly delightful experience for wine enthusiasts. The wine tasting selection is impressive, providing a wonderful opportunity to sample a variety of excellent vintages. Furthermore, a well-stocked shop allows visitors to purchase their favorite wines to enjoy at home.

the-gallery-at-grande-provence.author: Julian Govender
the-gallery-at-grande-provence.text: Wow! Lunch at Grande Provence was awesome. Peaceful atmosphere. Friendly staff. Beautiful and neat environment. Nature at its best. We followed the Blue Line Tram. Throughly emjoyed the experience. Highly recommended.

the-gallery-at-grande-provence.author: Anne-Marié Le Roux
the-gallery-at-grande-provence.text: This is one of my favorite wine farms! From the moment you arrive, you feel as if you are in a different country! It is beautiful, with a bistro amore formal restaurant and a gallery with artworks in the garden. This is a must-visit farm!

studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.author: Mpho Mojapelo
studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.text: This space is a haven for creativity and relaxation. As a creative, I find endless inspiration from the stunning paintings and captivating sculptures. It’s an incredible place to recharge and spark new ideas. Definitely worth the visit—you’ll find yourself wanting to come back again and again!

studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.author: Stephen Shearer (Satevo)
studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.text: Studio G is hands-down my favourite cannabis destination! The moment you step in, the vibe is on point—modern, welcoming, and absolutely chill. Their dab bar is a total game-changer. Whether you’re new to cannabis or are an experienced stoner the kind and friendly staff are always ready assist or guide you.

studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.author: Za Stevens
studio-g-art-gallery-creative-studio.text: I now know what I'm working hard in life for- to afford a Gerhard van Niekerk sculpture 🙂 The current art on display is mind blowing, looking forward to the upcoming watches!

ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.author: Jackie Constant
ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.text: I had a delicious strawberry and pineapple smoothy..i wanted gave the raw juice too... But was simply too full

ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.author: Andries Potgieter
ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.text: Lovely old theater, which is in need of an update, but cozy and small enough to ensure everybody has a good view of the artist during the show.... Only coffees are on sale at the theater, so make your dinner reservations elsewhere.

ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.author: laura
ou-meulteater-old-mill-theatre.text: Great little intimate theatre 🎭 Good seats, please add pop-up coffee bar ☕️ at intermission.

lionel-smit-studio.author: Stuart Hermansen
lionel-smit-studio.text: Recently went to visit the artist again. Such a peaceful vibe there, quiet, contained, very inspiring. Busy in his studio, painting for his next exhibition. His work is well displayed in the gallery, the garden is now well established, green, looking lovely, his sculptures at home in the landscape.

lionel-smit-studio.author: Celeste Storey
lionel-smit-studio.text: I am speechless! Magnificent art. It’s mind blowing to see the amount of love, care and detail that’s put into the sculptures and paintings. Definitely a must see!!

lionel-smit-studio.author: Jan-Henri Booyens
lionel-smit-studio.text: Beautiful bronze sculptures . Gorgeous open loft like studio very inspiring

s-n-chal-senekal.author: Alex Goldsmith
s-n-chal-senekal.text: My wife and I were traveling in South Africa for our honeymoon and were fortunate to walk into this gallery. The artwork is amazing and immediately blew us away. We worked with the galley to commission custom artwork from photos we took while on safari.

s-n-chal-senekal.author: Nicki Piek
s-n-chal-senekal.text: Wether you consider yourself an “art fundi” or not - this art gallery in Franschoek is an absolute must see! I very nearly walked straight into the glass doors as I stared at all these stunning works of art hanging on the wall!

s-n-chal-senekal.author: Nadja Cleven Moderatorin & Eventmanagement
s-n-chal-senekal.text: Sénéchal-Senekal Is a very beautiful art gallery in Franschhoek. We went to visit the Gallery during our Roadtrip trough South Africa and were totally exited to have found such a beautiful place and unique art pieces during our trip. The artist has an impressing collection of local art inspired by the South African nature and wildlife.

afrikaans-language-museum.author: Dan Zeeman
afrikaans-language-museum.text: The restored rooms on the first floor are lovely, giving us a glimpse into a time gone by. Just note that the museum in its entirety is geared more towards tour groups, so while there is ample space to move, the rooms feel a bit empty. I also wish that there were postcards or souvenirs available for purchase.

afrikaans-language-museum.author: Petri Steyn
afrikaans-language-museum.text: What an absolute experience revisiting our past in a single building and reading more about our rich Afrikaans language

afrikaans-language-museum.author: Carli Pienaar
afrikaans-language-museum.text: I absolutely loved viewing this place!

gus-gallery.author: Sascha-lee Stander
gus-gallery.text: I had my very first exhibition here this is such a spectacular gallery and lives up to its space and atmosphere

gus-gallery.author: Scott Eric Williams
gus-gallery.text: A great gallery situated in what used to be a church. The gallery, under the direction of curator-Valerie Geselev- has a wide range of programs that extend even beyond arts events. Well worth a visit if you're in Stellenbosch

gus-gallery.author: Jeandre Horn
gus-gallery.text: Old converted Church but not just a walk in venue, was there for an artist launch and what was on display was well curated.

bright-street-gallery.author: Claudette Downing
bright-street-gallery.text: Art beautiful and lovely coffeeshop, but art gallery smells like old sigarette smoke. Really not a pleasure if you're not a smoker.

bright-street-gallery.author: Judith Hilton-green
bright-street-gallery.text: Great art gallery and lovely outside area where one can enjoy a nice cuppa and relax with friends

bright-street-gallery.author: Andrew Lynott
bright-street-gallery.text: Quaint little gallery with local artists displaying their work. Well worth a visit

muse-gallery-franschhoek.author: Xandra Groenewold
muse-gallery-franschhoek.text: When you’re on your way to or leaving Grande Provence for wine and art do stop at Muse Gallery. What a briljant, inspiring gallery.

muse-gallery-franschhoek.author: Rose van der Merwe
muse-gallery-franschhoek.text: Wonderful fine art gallery! Very exciting work. Well worth a visit.

muse-gallery-franschhoek.author: GRAEME BOOTHMAN
muse-gallery-franschhoek.text: We fell across this place whilst out for a walk. Very picturesque location and has some beautiful sculptures in the gardens. The lady was super welcoming even though we said we wouldn't be buying, just browsing. Some amazing art inside from very talented locals but out of our price range. If you've got the cash, have a look.

carey-carter-sculpture.author: Marie Holler
carey-carter-sculpture.text: A wonderful art exhibition with truly stunning sculptures. Each piece is unique and full of character. The location itself is very special, and the artist brings something truly exceptional to the space. A memorable and inspiring experience — highly recommended.

carey-carter-sculpture.author: Guillaume Masson
carey-carter-sculpture.text: I simply love Carey Carter’s magnificent sculptures! Each piece radiates a profound appreciation for the beauty and complexity of the human form. Her busts and figurative works are breathtaking, full of grace, emotion, and exquisite detail.

carey-carter-sculpture.author: martha jane
carey-carter-sculpture.text: Gorgeous pieces that seem to emerge organically from the surrounding lush gardens. The work is surreal and ethereal, perfectly complemented by the studio’s scenic setting. A visit is absolutely worthwhile

ithemba-craftart-gallery.author: Arno Els
ithemba-craftart-gallery.text: Lovely little place with the cutest Dutch tannie that explained the history and art with so much love in her! Food was great ❤️

ithemba-craftart-gallery.author: Ian Gehrlich-Orr
ithemba-craftart-gallery.text: I love all the handmade art and the beautiful gallery. The environment felt very pleasant especially because the doors were left open making it feel like you were connected to the surrounding environment. I can't wait to go back and see what new art they have next time.

ithemba-craftart-gallery.author: Shane Settle
ithemba-craftart-gallery.text: This is a great spot with all sorts of hand-made artwork that really impressed me. They also have a Cafe built into the gallery, and the food and service was great!

babylonstoren-wine-museum.author: Mila Londt
babylonstoren-wine-museum.text: Truly was a very unique experience compared to most museums I've been to. It's very interactive, allowing you to actually engage with it's history in all sensory ways. It also makes use of some very creative artistic choices to truly enrich the experience.

babylonstoren-wine-museum.author: Chris Dixon
babylonstoren-wine-museum.text: We spent most of the day exploring the Babylonstoren estate. The setting is wonderful.There is something for everyone, the gardens are beautiful and very interesting to wander through. We joined the olive tour and tasting and thoroughly enjoyed the experience. Our guide provided us with lots of information.

babylonstoren-wine-museum.author: Peter Simon
babylonstoren-wine-museum.text: The Wine Tasting was Awesome, Cause of Kenzo. He is so kind ! Got Great experience! His Patience for this job jumps over to You! Thank you Kenzo! Awesome

gallery88.author: Lisa Mmmm
gallery88.text: I do not recommend going to this gallery, the owner or whoever that man that works there he is soo rude ! l was literally chased away like a dog, he is soo mean!! There are better art gallery up on the main road not this one!

gallery88.author: Yolanda Kritz
gallery88.text: The ideal venue. Upmarket and stylish with a hands on owner

gallery88.author: Mark Williams
gallery88.text: Great venue. Fantastic and interesting art. Highly recommended. Staff on point

redberry-farm.author: Esti Erasmus
redberry-farm.text: This must be the best kept property I have visited in a long time. Everything is in exceptional condition, the gardens are just beautiful, the food was delicious, the deli was amazing and the staff are well trained and helpful. Would highly recommend even if it is not strawberry season. Lovely visit for lunch and a stroll.

redberry-farm.author: Zharnel Burger
redberry-farm.text: Redberry Farm was such an incredible experience! We had so much fun exploring the maze—it was bigger and more challenging than we expected, and we loved every minute of it. Strawberry picking was another highlight, and there’s nothing better than enjoying fresh, sweet strawberries straight from the farm.

redberry-farm.author: claire
redberry-farm.text: What a fantastic place for kids and adults. We did the maze, a mini train ride and picked strawberries. Definitely a must see place. We could have spent a whole day exploring and enjoying the farm.

hillcrest-berry-orchards.author: Kalysha Klaassen
hillcrest-berry-orchards.text: The most beautiful fluffy airy little clouds of scones! Honestly this was the best scone I have ever eaten, literally melt in the mouth. Had no idea Hillcrest had this many flavors of jam , every single little jam was delicious but the favorite was cherry as well as raspberry. Staff and waiters were exceptionally polite and friendly.

hillcrest-berry-orchards.author: Broney Solomons
hillcrest-berry-orchards.text: Still the best scones ever!! Was enjoying it with my now two favorite jams although it was a struggle to choose at first because there's a lot!! .. My friendly waitress Ronelle was a big help specially with the lunch nd some dessert we appreciate it!

hillcrest-berry-orchards.author: R.
hillcrest-berry-orchards.text: It's always been a favorite. The menu is vast, varied & excellent, with prices matching the ambiance & seemingly from an earlier time. The view is one of the Wineland's best. Servers are perfect, prompt & friendly. Knocking it out of park, one of today's specials was fresh raspberries with cream or ice cream. It tasted like summer.

helderberg-farm.author: Karon Wiercx
helderberg-farm.text: Old world charm. Lovely established trees, garden and forests. Playground equipment is a bit old and petting farm has few animals. Favourite place to walk my dog then chill for coffee

helderberg-farm.author: Francesca
helderberg-farm.text: Beautiful views and stunning location. Farm is very well kept, quiet, private and pretty. Their website is also very informative and helpful. The strawberries are delicious and smell amazing. Unfortunately, I was rather disappointed with the actual picking experience.

helderberg-farm.author: Nicole Olivier
helderberg-farm.text: Visited Helderberg Farm to do the 4x4 TRAIL. Entrance fee is paid at the teagarden of R250 per vehicle. Beautiful views from the viewpoint. If you choose the red 4x4 trail at the top of the rocky area keep left if there is a water puddle, someone else got stuck there while we were there it's quite a drop in the water.

the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.author: Marikie Theron
the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.text: Private camping ground on a berry farm west of Swellendam. Managed by the owners. Water on tap, power. Swim in a pool or a dam. Walk, hike, foofy slide or just sit and enjoy the view. Part of the Hermitage Meander.

the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.author: Monique Whelan
the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.text: Working berry farm with beautiful views! I’d drive here just for the blueberry pie ! The ladies in the kitchen are incredibly kind and hospitable! You can do day visits or just go to their waterfront restaurant if you’re not planning on staying. We did camp here and it wasn’t bad. Lovely spot and owners are attentive.

the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.author: SARA PEREZ RODRIGUEZ
the-berry-guest-farm-swellendam.text: The farm is lovely and located in a beautiful spot just at the bottom of the mountains with stunning views. They have a good flat paddock to pitch your tent or whatever you got to camp. Restaurant has great home made food and affordable. The best, the people who host.

outeniqua-game-farm.author: peter mills
outeniqua-game-farm.text: Astounding and Exhilarating. It was the most unbelievable experience. Jurg Olsen, Ben Bode and Gill Clarke were our amazing Guides through the whole experience, The briefing was to the Point and very Very Clear. Explaining that a Lion is always a Lion and to be respected at all times.

outeniqua-game-farm.author: David Botha
outeniqua-game-farm.text: We got married at Outenique Game Farm on 21 February 2026, and honestly, it was the most incredible day of our lives. From the moment we arrived, it felt like home. The team went completely above and beyond to make our day special. Nothing felt like a “service” — we were treated like family.

outeniqua-game-farm.author: Leo Dz
outeniqua-game-farm.text: We did the Walk of Lions today and it was an absolutely amazing experience. Seeing lions this close in such a natural setting was truly breathtaking. The guides were fantastic and very attentive to the lions’ behavior, always reacting appropriately to what the animals needed.

strawberry-king.author: M PK
strawberry-king.text: Strawberry King. You gonna be kidding…. Cheaters King for sure! Cheaters, cheaters and more time cheaters! On November 1st, I bought a 2-kilo box of strawberries. They looked fresh and healthy. I ate a few on my way back to Cape Town, and they were.

strawberry-king.author: Sandhya Petersen
strawberry-king.text: Fresh produce and all strawberry products from syrups to jams to frozen berries.

strawberry-king.author: danischa modler
strawberry-king.text: Absolutely love your strawberries 🍓 The staff is always friendly. Thank you for making my day when I pop in.

rocklands-farm.author: Emmylou Allan
rocklands-farm.text: What a stunning spot. We had a breakfast date under tree with a beautiful view. The background sounds were chickens and goats. The service was friendly, the coffee and homemade condensed milk was Amazing! I had avo toast and my hubby had a sloppy Joe. The side salad was literally out of the garden and the dressing was so yummy.

rocklands-farm.author: Mary Banks
rocklands-farm.text: Terrible single track road especially trying to pass other cars especially with boats behind. Uncomfortable seating. Food expensive. First visit. Lovely staff. Bought flowers and brinjals. Nice setting. Sorry won't return. I like your social media but my first visit was disappointing.

rocklands-farm.author: Jonathan T
rocklands-farm.text: Cute goats and fresh eggs! Theresa was extremely friendly and kind. The eggs aren't cheap... But it's nice to meet the chickens doing the hard work and know that the eggs didn't come from a factory farm. Be warned: there's a 600 metre dirt laneway you drive on to enter which is fairly treacherous if you don't drive a 4x4.

indigo-berries.author: Boikie Peme
indigo-berries.text: Always good to experience life on that side of the world. I've always seen Berries on packages, it was great to see Berries on trees.

garden-of-eden-berries.author: Sandra Lavender
garden-of-eden-berries.text: I purchased strawberries for R39.99 from Spar Waterfall Durban on Tuesday the 16th Feb 2021. They just had Garden of Eden on the label so I dont know if you were the actual supplier to the Spar . I came to eat them on Thursday, 2 days later, and everyone of them had gone mouldy and I couldn't eat them.

garden-of-eden-berries.author: Christiaan van Veijeren
garden-of-eden-berries.text: Nice strawberry picking just a pity you can't eat in the field 🍓

garden-of-eden-berries.author: John Webster (Parajon)
garden-of-eden-berries.text: Strawberry farm for visitors Sept to dec. But unmade road is bad for maybe 4kms.

appleberry-farm.author: Chris Clark
appleberry-farm.text: Wonderful trail run through the forest and orchard

wild-oats-community-farmers-market.author: Jeff Morse
wild-oats-community-farmers-market.text: Absolutely MUST attend if you're ever in the area! It's only open from 8 AM - 12 PM on Saturday mornings. Be there early ...it's an absolute local blast!!! Great food and locally made products!! Jeff Morse Connecticut, USA

wild-oats-community-farmers-market.author: Daniel Tran
wild-oats-community-farmers-market.text: Visit: November 2025 Such a lovely farmers market, with another craft market so close by. Love the amount of food variety there is here. There's so much to try and so many different and unique stalls. Our favorite was the Cape Oysters stall, with very cheap oysters.

wild-oats-community-farmers-market.author: Tono
wild-oats-community-farmers-market.text: Visited the Sedgefield Market on Saturday and absolutely loved it. Very busy, but with a great atmosphere. There are three well-supported markets with an excellent variety of stalls, especially food. The vibe is fantastic. The only downside is the long queues for food, but that just shows how popular it is.

harkerville-saturday-market.author: Francois vd Merwe
harkerville-saturday-market.text: Market was great, the food and drinks section was the standout. There were a few generic "this is Africa" stall selling the same carvings and souvenirs you can get at any market or bizaar in SA. But there was also some real traditional and authentic stuff. I highly recommend full Monty breakfast plates.

harkerville-saturday-market.author: Danella Cassel
harkerville-saturday-market.text: Harkerville Saturday Market is a MUST VISIT. Not only is the food amazing but there is a wide variety of it available. There’s food and snacks to suit everyone’s needs. There’s also a variety of different shops whether it’s books,plants,art or even cutlery. There’s no way you can get bored.

harkerville-saturday-market.author: Lesha de Wet
harkerville-saturday-market.text: Lovely market with a foods section and a crafts section. Both are quite large so there are many options. We had de most delicious breakfast by combining foods from different stalls. Would definitely recommend coming here on time because it can get quite busy. There is also a nice playground for kids.

klondyke-cherry-farm.author: teagan young
klondyke-cherry-farm.text: We spent the better part of 2 hours wondering through the beautiful cherry trees as well as some appel and pear trees. Our whole experience was amazing and it all started and ended with the young woman that greeted us at the gate and explained all the rules.

klondyke-cherry-farm.author: miah
klondyke-cherry-farm.text: I loved my stay here the cottage was very cozy and the electric blankets were a nice touch. The view was beautiful. The only complaint I have is that the light bulbs in the cottage were too bright and did not suit the atmosphere of the cottage, I would suggest replacing them with warming lighting. Overall very pleasant experience!

klondyke-cherry-farm.author: Charlotte Spears
klondyke-cherry-farm.text: We came at the right time, most of the cherries could be reached without using a ladder, and they were very sweet. But the price to take the cherries away has already risen to be higher than what you can buy in the supermarket. It's a long way from Cape Town, and it would be worthwhile to spend the whole day here if you have enough time.

langkloof-roses.author: Kayla Henning
langkloof-roses.text: We had the most incredible wedding weekend at Langkloof Roses. The setting is absolutely beautiful — a perfect mix of nature and modern elegance. The team went above and beyond, making us feel calm and supported every step of the way.

langkloof-roses.author: Mianca Esterhuizen
langkloof-roses.text: The venue is beautiful, lovely for wedding use. The staff is lovely and meals are great. We were just there mid April 2026 and there is a bit of an issue with theft. 2-3 units reported that they had things stolen from the room and the police came and found some of the stolen items.

langkloof-roses.author: Elmary Cathy Kuiler
langkloof-roses.text: In March I tried so hard to make a booking for a birthday party that was in April, the person in charge even gave her personal number but after several attempts of trying to get hold of her I called their office and according to her her phone was stolen but she will get back to me.

engelke-flowers.author: Carmen Joshua
engelke-flowers.text: Fabulous, freshly cut flowers✂️🌷🌻Grown right there on the farm! Excellent variety & well-stocked @ pocket-friendly prices💳 This florist shop has the potential to earn more five-star ratings, thereby retaining existing clients and attracting new ones.

engelke-flowers.author: Michelle Thomas
engelke-flowers.text: Bought fresh flowers for our grandma and mother's grave. They have a variety of products and love the sunflowers. They cater for all your flower needs, especially the mothers day flowers. They are budget friendly, love the variety of proteas. Their prices for proteas range from R50 to R60 a bunch etc

engelke-flowers.author: MANFRED BOSWELL
engelke-flowers.text: Excellent price and a large choice of flowers.

canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.author: Nicola Stevenson
canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.text: Beautiful, peaceful lavender fields. With the cutest gift shop with the most amazing soaps and nicnaks. You can also get something to drink at their small cafe and do wine tasting. The views are stunning! The staff are all super friendly. Definitely recommended!

canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.author: ricky uys
canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.text: The lavender picking at Canettevallei is Bucket List stuff! Memorable! I also ordered gifts from Canettevallei and the entire process was seamless and magical. The unboxing of my order was amazing! Thank you Canettevallei. Iove everything about you!

canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.author: Wayne Dean
canettevallei-lavender-and-wine-farm.text: Beautiful experience with the wife and daughter. Picked lavender with family in beautiful surroundings. Quant little coffee shop and seating and a tree over looking the lank. Stunning!

japhtas-flower-farm.author: Andrew Vermaak
japhtas-flower-farm.text: Great place for getting beautiful flowers. A pity they don't look after there ducks and chickens like they do the plants. Small enclosure and filthy water... Do better

japhtas-flower-farm.author: Lude Tang
japhtas-flower-farm.text: I got a graduation bouquet from this lovely flower farm in Cape Town, and it was such a great experience. The bouquet was for my boyfriend’s mom, and she absolutely loved it! The flowers are beautiful, warm, and full of fresh, vibrant flowers. They wrapped the bouquet on the spot, and you can really tell the flowers were freshly picked.

japhtas-flower-farm.author: Tarryn Foot
japhtas-flower-farm.text: Beautiful flower farm. Kind staff. I got a stunning bouquet.

the-wild-bunch-flower-farm.author: Matt Weisse
the-wild-bunch-flower-farm.text: What a lovely place on the Bainskloof Mountain Side. Indeginous Flower and Herbs galore. Bunches of wild flowers and fresh Mountain Air. A hidden gem to be discovered.

chart-farm.author: Mary Banks
chart-farm.text: I was a little bit dubious because I had recently read a few reviews about slow service. Today they did their best...there were only 2 waitrons on duty and a Manageress. The food was plentiful and tasty. The views and location is pleasing

chart-farm.author: Anne-Marie Dunn
chart-farm.text: The views is stunning. Very relaxed chill vibe. But the waitering was pathetic. Had to send milkshake back. My quiche Lorraine filling was not baked properly. Didnt send back as waiter barely there. Didn’t want to order cake if my first order was not good. The picking roses is a nice idea but I would do it before 12pm as it was Very hot.

chart-farm.author: Nicola Stevenson
chart-farm.text: Beautiful views and surroundings. Gorgeous roses with loads of different colours and varieties. There is also a cute cafe that we didn’t get to try but hopefully will next time

cheverells-farm.author: Alicia Nel
cheverells-farm.text: Cheverells Farm is such a beautiful place to get married. The farm setting is peaceful and charming, with open spaces and lovely views that make the day feel warm and relaxed. It has that perfect blend of rustic character and laid-back elegance, which creates a really special atmosphere.

cheverells-farm.author: Neil G RC
cheverells-farm.text: Our second visit to Cheverells Farm, this time staying in the beautiful Libertas House. The accommodation surpassed our expectations, the house is magnificent and perfect for one or more families enjoying a getaway break on the farm.

cheverells-farm.author: Iain Lovatt
cheverells-farm.text: A tranquil stop on the way and well worth the detour. The location and stunning views, warm welcome, great coffee and fresh food make this a perfect spot to rest, revive and carry on. The photographers don’t do the views just but the coffee and breakfast are a taste sensation

hoogwater-farm.author: Seelan Govindasamy
hoogwater-farm.text: Beautiful farm, with figs, cling peaches, pears. Prepare a picnic bag and enjoy your day picking fruits. Check when the farm is open and the time.

hoogwater-farm.author: Hoosain Narker
hoogwater-farm.text: Visited there 3 years ago with my girls. Enjoyed picking peaches, and could bring home quite some to share. Good for an outing with family.

hoogwater-farm.author: Riyaaz Fakier
hoogwater-farm.text: The place is amazing, and my family and I really enjoyed our time there. The house is nice and big with enough space for large families. The kids really enjoyed playing in the pool and then dipping in the wood fired hot tub. There is also a dam where you can fish and it has amazing views.

robberg-nature-reserve.author: Rosalie Wilder
robberg-nature-reserve.text: Awesome day at Robberg Nature Preserve—one of the most exhilarating hikes I’ve done in ages (August 1, 2025). Some of the trail is little more than a whisper along the cliffside: narrow, exposed, and thrillingly wild. At points there’s barely room for your feet as the path hugs the edge, with sheer drops straight to the ocean below.

robberg-nature-reserve.author: Narisha Maharaj
robberg-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful vistas. Entrance e fee of R75 pp. When parking is full you have to park on the road. They have clean toilets and a small food kiosk at the entrance to the trails that serves cold drinks and coffee and African walking sticks. 3 different trails, the gap is 2.2km and the hardest 9km. We did the 2.2km walk , rocky but manageable.

robberg-nature-reserve.author: Marcus Ladds
robberg-nature-reserve.text: Absolutely gorgeous views and stunning nature. Be aware, the walk is not always easy - do not try to do this in flip flops or sandals. Wear proper hiking shoes with good grip, and be aware of some steep drops and areas close to the cliff edge.

jonkershoek-nature-reserve.author: Jorg Mauderer
jonkershoek-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful clean and safe trails,no traffic,scenic and lots of running streams(July),very beautiful waterfalls ( in Dec very meagre stream,but that is our summer season ) lovely minimalist restaurant.......I improved my fitness,stress-relieved and had a awesome day out with my daughter......lots more to follow

jonkershoek-nature-reserve.author: Daria
jonkershoek-nature-reserve.text: Magical place! The views are out of this world. The trails are in great condition and very well signposted. The access was easy and straightforward and I loved the coffee place at the entrance to the park. Will come back to do more trails for sure!

jonkershoek-nature-reserve.author: Marelize Bezuidenhout
jonkershoek-nature-reserve.text: Only a limited number of vehicles are allowed into the park. If you’re permitted to enter with your vehicle, you can save about 5 km one way on the hike to the waterfalls. Otherwise, you can park at the gate (card payment is available at the entrance, or you can book online) and hike from there on.

tygerberg-nature-reserve.author: Jacques du Toit
tygerberg-nature-reserve.text: The Superstay.io team loves hiking at Tygerberg Nature Reserve. Some of our favourite trails include: - The Tortoise Trail - The Grey Bok Trail - The Uketshe Trail (is by far our favourite, as it is the longest and most scenic) The staff are friendly, getting in is easy and cheap, and the reserve is very clean and well maintained.

tygerberg-nature-reserve.author: Claire Kolbe
tygerberg-nature-reserve.text: Lovely early morning hike in this reserve. It really is beautiful all year around. I took my team for a hike and breakfast at the top. We all thoroughly enjoyed it. The range is from easier hikes to slightly more challenging.

tygerberg-nature-reserve.author: Karen Pretorius
tygerberg-nature-reserve.text: An amazing piece of nature in the north with breathtaking views of Blouberg, Robben Island and Table Mountain. Hiking trails for various levels of fitness. Our group ranged in age from 9 years to over 70 and everyone coped well with the route. Entry is R40, discount for pensioners and an annual fee is available for regular visitors.

alphen-trail.author: Yumna Begg
alphen-trail.text: It's a beautiful trail with loads of routes to chose from. I do wish people were more considerate with picking up after their pets but nonetheless it's a beautiful outdoor walk.

alphen-trail.author: Rika Burger
alphen-trail.text: Beautiful trail. Very clean and safe even for solo female walkers/runners. Fair bit of people on the trail and even some dogs and horses. Everyone very friendly. On Saturdays the Parkrun is held at 8am. Dose Coffee Project supplies great coffees here on Saturday mornings 🤩

alphen-trail.author: Jawhara Suliman
alphen-trail.text: The Alphen Trail came highly recommended by both family members and friends, and it certainly lived up to the praise. From the moment I started walking, I could understand why so many people speak so highly of it. The atmosphere was welcoming, with people walking, running, and enjoying the outdoors.

robberg-hiking-trail.author: Monika Karlińska
robberg-hiking-trail.text: Amazing place and must to see in SA The Robberg hiking trail is one of the most scenic and popular trails on the famous Garden Route in southern South Africa. Located in Plettenberg Bay, it offers fantastic views of the peninsula and the entire nature reserve, the surrounding cliffs, white beaches, and the ocean.

robberg-hiking-trail.author: Dermot Higgins
robberg-hiking-trail.text: The most beautiful hike I’ve ever done. The long walk to the point and back is quite challenging as it involves navigating a good portion over rocky areas, lots of steps and hill climbing. It’s imperative to have good footwear for grip and support plus water.

robberg-hiking-trail.author: Tessa Viaene
robberg-hiking-trail.text: Definitely one of the most beautiful hikes I have ever done. It can be quite challenging and requires a moderate level of fitness. The trail is not always well-developed, and at times you need to climb over large rocks. Despite the demanding sections, the breathtaking views make it absolutely worth the effort.

east-head-view-point.author: Gregory Hing
east-head-view-point.text: Knysna East Head is an absolute must visit, especially at sunset. Watching the sun slowly dip below the horizon while casting golden reflections across the lagoon was truly magical. The views are breathtaking, with dramatic cliffs, crashing waves, and endless ocean vistas creating the perfect backdrop.

east-head-view-point.author: Peter Teitge
east-head-view-point.text: This is one of my favourite spots in the entire country. The views are absolutely breath taking. There are several view points, each offering a different view of the Knysna Heads. It is a short, easy walk to access all the viewing decks. Some of the decks have (paid for) binoculars which one can use. The decks are well maintained.

east-head-view-point.author: Martin Stevens
east-head-view-point.text: The Knysna Heads East Viewpoint offers one of the most iconic and rewarding views in the Garden Route. From this elevated lookout, you get a sweeping panorama over the Knysna Lagoon as it meets the Indian Ocean, with the Western Head clearly visible across the narrow channel.

blaauwberg-nature-reserve.author: Rafique Begg
blaauwberg-nature-reserve.text: Not on everyone’s radar, but The Two Hills Trail is a popular 7.3 km guided hiking route in the Blaauwberg Nature Reserve . Not too tough, but full of colonial history. Check out Friends of the Blaauwberg on Facebook to arrange a group hike.

blaauwberg-nature-reserve.author: Zubaida Kumandan
blaauwberg-nature-reserve.text: It was a lovely hike of two hills with our guide Natali. Beautiful views on top of the hill of Table Mountain and scenery. On the other hill it was an amazing view of Robben Island and the Blaawberg area. It came with a bit of history so that made it even better .

blaauwberg-nature-reserve.author: Nicole Wahl
blaauwberg-nature-reserve.text: Very nice and stunning views of table mountain. Not expensive and a very nice place to picnic and braai. Just check the weather and wind, otherwise it would be very cold. Went there to watch the flowers and there were a lot of flowers and it was beautiful

majik-forest.author: Fuwaad Mahmood
majik-forest.text: Wonderful park with short hiking trails, a little dam and serene views, there is also a kids play area and absolutely amazing place for a picnic, parking can be a little difficult however there is another entrance in the adjacent mall in which you may park in as well, lawns are well maintained and place is well kept.

majik-forest.author: Leandre de Jager
majik-forest.text: Beautiful, free to enter nature reserve. The people are super friendly and might just strike up conversation. The water is much clearer than anticipated and the fish are beautiful and healthy. Majik Forest is pet friendly (please keep your dogs leashed) and the forest is absolutely breathtaking. I frequent often, but not often enough ❤️

majik-forest.author: James Best
majik-forest.text: Safe and great for the family. Nice lake at the top with some fish in it. A wooded section with some shade. About 30 minutes from entrance at the bottom to the top entrance. Nice picnic spots with well kept grass. Really beautiful views of the mountains.

fernkloof-nature-reserve.author: Terri-leigh Stopford
fernkloof-nature-reserve.text: Fernkloof is a well looked after nature reserve. It is bin free, so you need to keep your trash with you until the end. The visitor's centre offers information about the different Fauna and Flora in the reserve as well as free trail walking sticks (must be returned at the end of the trail).

fernkloof-nature-reserve.author: Daleen Myburgh
fernkloof-nature-reserve.text: Great, beautiful hike. We got there at 06:30 am, we were alone and unsure what hike to do. We started for the easy, short walk to the waterfall. Very small waterfall. Then we turned into Lemoenkop hike to get some heights and a view of hermanus. Sunrise coming over the mountain was amazing. Lemoenkop took about 90 min to do.

fernkloof-nature-reserve.author: Priscilla Huet
fernkloof-nature-reserve.text: Fernkloof is a truly stunning nature reserve with a variety of hiking trails for all levels. We chose the Red Trail to the waterfall, which we completed in about 90 minutes. The route was not only enjoyable but rewarded us with absolutely beautiful views of the mountains and surrounding landscape.

de-hoop-nature-reserve.author: Mandi Kotze
de-hoop-nature-reserve.text: We had a lovely time camping at this amazing place. Went cycling, saw zebras, ostriches, eland, bontebok. Had a lovely 5 star quality lunch while looking at the ocean. Breakfast was also excellent. The ladies at the office were extremely helpful in assisting us with urgent documents that needed to be sent.

de-hoop-nature-reserve.author: Bernadine O'Donoghue
de-hoop-nature-reserve.text: We did the Whale Trail in the De Hoop Nature Reserve. Breathtaking views and brilliant accommodation along the trail. The huts were neat, clean, and had all the vasic amenities that we needed.

de-hoop-nature-reserve.author: Jenny Van Staaden
de-hoop-nature-reserve.text: What an amazing experience! The trail wasn’t too tough for me, but fitness levels will vary. The scenery is absolutely breathtaking, and the huts are well-equipped—except for the last one, which is more basic. Be sure to stock up on supplies before entering the reserve. I’m so grateful to live in such a stunning country.

kogelberg-nature-reserve.author: Antofogasta Ashantee
kogelberg-nature-reserve.text: Kogelberg Nature Reserve is well maintained and I love what they did on the gravel road from entrance leading to the reception. Making such beauty of nature accessible with any type of car. No limits. The markings of trails are clear and their cabins are breathtaking.

kogelberg-nature-reserve.author: Tony
kogelberg-nature-reserve.text: Nature ar it's best. Clearly marked trails. Reserve in good order. Friendly staff. Perfect for a leisurely meander or a good trail run

kogelberg-nature-reserve.author: Kathleen Nigrini
kogelberg-nature-reserve.text: Six of us ladies stayed in Silver Pagoda for the weekend. The Friday evening we braaid, Saturday the rain came down, but luckily only after we had been for a walk that dished up incredible vista of the mountains, fynbos and river. The linen is high quality, the beds comfy, and even the compost toilet works fantastically!

west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.author: Juanita Aitkenhead
west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.text: Dont be confused. The 5 stars is for what God has blessed us with. As for this National Park, management, its gone from hero to zero in 5 years. The private consessions are thriving, but not this SANPARKS. The signage explains it all. And Kraalbaai is now a deserted construction site. Iconic Geelbek now deserted too.

west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.author: Barefoot mart
west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.text: Very special park. Bird watchers blinds were good, small hikes. A lot depends on the the time of day and time of year. Lots of flowers in their rainy/winter season.

west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.author: Nadia Yarrington
west-coast-national-park-marine-protecte.text: My first experience at West Coast National Park. I thoroughly enjoyed this beautiful, pristine & well-kept place. Will definitely be back for hikes & accommodation in the future. My only issue was the LONG wait to get in.

point-of-human-origins.author: Aranganathan S
point-of-human-origins.text: Must visit cave. Only to 1 cave, we are allowed to enter with guide. We need to pay before the trip via online. After parking the car, they take us to bottom by van. We need to hike down and up on wooden steps. Guide was too good in explaining. One of icons of mossel bay.

point-of-human-origins.author: Geoffrey Berg
point-of-human-origins.text: Great to see the cave after the amazing exhibition in the cape of good hope, seeing how the other half live in Pinnacle Point was also interesting (amazing how far we've come from dwelling in caves to gated golf communities 🤢).

point-of-human-origins.author: Shawn Reed
point-of-human-origins.text: Extraordinary tour. I booked it without knowing what it was. I thought it would just be a nice hike to some place. But I was beyond surprised to see stuff made by ancient people in the wall of the cave that was over 100,000 years old.

haweqwa-nature-reserve.author: Candice Knipe
haweqwa-nature-reserve.text: It is absolutely beautiful here. The waters generally reached above the knee but there are some deeper spots where you'll find yourself unable to stand. I completed part of the Rockhopper trail and took a detour to a popular small waterfall. We were warned about baboons but did not see any on the hike.

haweqwa-nature-reserve.author: Unique Love
haweqwa-nature-reserve.text: This was hands down one of the most thrilling, beautiful, and unforgettable hikes I’ve ever done. About 2.5 hours from Cape Town, this six-hour journey was filled with fresh air, scenic trails, and breathtaking views. The best part? It ended with a hidden gem a secret waterfall.

haweqwa-nature-reserve.author: Iggy Zee
haweqwa-nature-reserve.text: Absolutely amazing. Definitely one of the most beautiful hikes I've ever done. It's about 1hr 20mins to get to the final pool. Once you get there, you will surely be amazed. 5 star hiking experience...

the-pipe-track.author: David Matlhoma
the-pipe-track.text: Did the Pipe Track hike and really enjoyed it. The trail is well-marked and easy to follow, with amazing views the whole way. It’s a relaxed hike with a nice mix of city, mountain, and ocean scenery. Perfect if you want something scenic without it being too intense. Would definitely do it again.

the-pipe-track.author: Maleeba Mwatsikesimbe
the-pipe-track.text: Pipe Track is one of Cape Town’s most underrated treasures. Winding along the western slopes of Table Mountain, this historic trail offers panoramic views of Camps Bay, the Twelve Apostles, and the Atlantic Ocean—all while tracing the old water pipeline laid in 1887.

the-pipe-track.author: Kittisak Lanlongsa
the-pipe-track.text: This is one of the best spots if you enjoy walking or running while taking in stunning views of Lion’s Head, Camps Bay, and Table Mountain. I went during sunset, and it was absolutely perfect. I highly recommend this place—whether you’re staying in Cape Town long-term or visiting for the first time, make sure to add it to your list.

goukamma-nature-reserve.author: Martina Behr
goukamma-nature-reserve.text: Just stunning place. So calm and perfect for a gateway. We were in Otters cottage which is positioned towards sunset which were spectacular. Private patio with braai was great and in the evening there was big fireplace in the cottage. Great hiking trails and kayaking for all level adventurers.

goukamma-nature-reserve.author: Paul KINNEAR
goukamma-nature-reserve.text: Wonderful area with various different options for hiking, including spectacular walk along the extensive sandy beaches of the Indian Ocean. Friendly staff and secure parking within the complex. Fun to cross the river on DIY ferry on pulley system. Picnic tables near the office on by the river. Idyllic !

goukamma-nature-reserve.author: A C
goukamma-nature-reserve.text: Had a 3 day stay with the family at this reserve. Originally booked a larger cottage, but was moved to a smaller one due to renovations. Ended up staying at Blacktail cottage, which is quite dated. Knowing more now, would opt for either of the other smaller ones as they come with wooden decks, vs sandy outside area.

featherbed-nature-reserve.author: Jürgen F.
featherbed-nature-reserve.text: The Featherbed Eco Experience in Knysna was an absolutely exceptional highlight of our trip. From the very beginning, everything was perfectly organized, and the staff stood out with their genuine warmth, attentiveness, and professionalism – they made us feel welcome and well looked after at all times.

featherbed-nature-reserve.author: Geoffrey Berg
featherbed-nature-reserve.text: I'm not sure I'd call it a nature reserve, but our guide was funny, informative and rather sweet. The views are exceptional, lunch was great, and the whole experience with the boat trip was very relaxing.

featherbed-nature-reserve.author: viC Bangamwabo
