featherbed-nature-reserve.text: My wife and I took the Featherbed ferry across the lagoon, got driven up near the Knysna Heads, then enjoyed a scenic downhill hike back to the Featherbed restaurant. The guide was friendly, funny, and really knowledgeable. The ferry ride back through the lagoon was the perfect finish - beautiful views and a smooth, well-run experienc

fransmanshoek-conservancy.author: Chantal Swart
fransmanshoek-conservancy.text: We were so lucky to be there on a day with very few people around. Then we got the most incredible surprise. A pod of dolphins, easily over 200, were feeding in the bay. There were moms and babies and it was absolutely mind blowing and so beautiful to watch. We spent such a long time just sitting there, completely in awe. 🐬

fransmanshoek-conservancy.author: Peter Teitge
fransmanshoek-conservancy.text: Fransmanshoek is an absolute must visit for any nature lover. The scenery here is breathtaking with endless views over the ocean. There are several view points. We also managed to see a few animals while we were here. There is a bit of a walk to get to the view points and besides being a bit uneven, it is an easy walk.

fransmanshoek-conservancy.author: Brian van Niekerk
fransmanshoek-conservancy.text: Absolutely beautiful, walking shows is jy an absolute must. There are so many different places to explore and it's quite difficult to do so with flip-flops.

kloof-corner.author: MW & Partner
kloof-corner.text: Great hike for sunset. Many people bring food and drinks to enjoy the sunset. We got food "toGo" shortly before from an indian place at seapoint called "the tandoor". And it was still warm when we arrived at the top. Just dont forget to ask for cuttlery. The food is super tasty. Everything freshly made by the owner.

kloof-corner.author: Brett Schlesinger
kloof-corner.text: It’s the most incredible walk … very manageable if you go up india venster to the cable car, but be careful there are walks around the kloof corner side, yhat start getting pretty technical… it’s also quite a strenuous walk of a couple of hours, so don’t do it unless you are fairly fit

kloof-corner.author: Salai arun CA
kloof-corner.text: This is a very short and beautiful. We were unlucky with the weather on the day we hiked. We were surrounded by mist. Yet this was a great hike to remember.

sanp-garden-of-eden.author: Evan Swart
sanp-garden-of-eden.text: See alot of tourists complain about the price for such a short walk but we paid SA citizen prices so not too bad if you just want to take a nice short stroll (20-30 mins/1km) and spot an array of fungi, birds, spiders or the vary scarce/shy animals.

sanp-garden-of-eden.author: Marion Geach
sanp-garden-of-eden.text: Safe wooden walkways through the forest . The dank smell of the undergrowth, Knysna louries flitting through the trees, little streams , emerald green ferns and tall ancient trees reaching for the sun make for a very memorable walk .

sanp-garden-of-eden.author: Claire Kolbe
sanp-garden-of-eden.text: Beautiful walk in the indigenous forests. Amazing wildlife, variety of huge trees and plants. Really tranquil walking around the forest. The. Boardwalks are well done with wire mesh to help when it gets slippery which is quite clever. Really loved our visit.

hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.author: Kay Adonis
hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.text: Nothing beats a scenic walk. Do I have to say more😍. The flowers, the cliffs, the dassies and birds, beautiful with something to look at with every step. My favourite part of going to Hermanus. A definite must💯

hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.author: tracy joseph
hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.text: Lovely walk. Fantastic views. Many well placed seating areas. A must do activity

hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.author: Oliver Krantz
hermanus-cliff-path-biodiversity-walk.text: This is a really epic walk. The best part of the walk is from town up the coast (ocean on your right) and that is about 2/3 of the total length. The beginning of the walk from new harbour is often n very close to the road and houses, so not that great.

klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.author: Naadiyah Johnson
klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.text: Klaasenbosch is an absolute gem for anyone who loves nature, tranquility, and a bit of outdoor family time. This serene greenbelt offers a peaceful escape, with a gentle stream running through lush greenery and plenty of shady spots, perfect for those warm summer days.

klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.author: Paul Richard
klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.text: Lovely trail. Easily accessible to everyone. Lots of small paths leading in various directions and commuting. In the forest, so plenty of shade (perfect for summer days). If you want a longer walk you can connect Klaasenbosch Greenbelt and Alphen trails together. Great place for a leisurely family day!

klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.author: Adrian Maasch
klaasenbosch-greenbelt-trail.text: What a beautiful walk to take the mind off everything and enjoy nature. The gentle stream (saw Swee Waxbills amongst the reeds) is loved by the dogs, make sure to bring a towel if yours enjoys the water.

grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.author: Ashen Ramnath
grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.text: A must visit for nature lovers! What a beautiful, scenic getaway. Well setup glamping units with all the necessary equipment. The hot tubs were great while star gazing under the night sky. Hikes through the Redwoods forest and the canopy views were amazing. I loved this spot!

grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.author: Erika Grobbelaar
grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.text: The forrest is an amazing experience. The glamping could be cleaner. We enjoyed our stay. The stars was a clear as can be.

grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.author: Carolin Petersen
grootvadersbosch-nature-reserve.text: Grootvadersbosch offers clean comfortable chalets well equipped. Hiking through beautiful forests with Redwoods planted back in 1902 reaching 40m to the canopy. By contrast the fynbos paths offer a varity of beautul flora.

cecilia-table-mountain.author: Phett L
cecilia-table-mountain.text: It is absolutely stunning. The area is so big you could hike for hours and still not go everywhere 😍

cecilia-table-mountain.author: Daniyal Khan
cecilia-table-mountain.text: The Cecelia Forest Hiking Trail is a hidden gem in Cape Town, offering a peaceful escape into nature just a stone's throw from the bustling city. I recently visited this trail with my friends, and it quickly became one of our favorite hiking spots.

cecilia-table-mountain.author: Grant Van Niekerk
cecilia-table-mountain.text: Tough but amazing trail, especially when you get to the dam,s. Little to no signage on the trail, so got a bit lost for a moment.

koeberg-nature-reserve.author: Clive Potgieter
koeberg-nature-reserve.text: Excellent, safe and secluded venue for mountain biking or hiking, with different routes covering shorter or longer distances, up to about 20km. My favourite retreat for me time. There are mild elevations, but no hectic climbing. It is usually quiet and there is a wide array of wildlife to be seen from the trails.

koeberg-nature-reserve.author: Peaceman Mashamba
koeberg-nature-reserve.text: Managed by Eskom. It is inside the Eskom Koeberg Nuclear Power Station. It is a great place for mountain biking and hiking. The trails are clearly marked. The trails are non technical and mostly hard packed and ideal for beginners. I usually go there for mountain biking.

koeberg-nature-reserve.author: Roy MacGregor
koeberg-nature-reserve.text: Very bushy & sandy area adjacent to the Koeberg Nuclear Power Station. Wild animals and birds roam freely. Access is strictly for persons carrying an approved activity ID card with photo. Hiking and cycling is only permitted on approved paths.

gerickes-point.author: Rosalie Wilder
gerickes-point.text: Gericke’s Point is a gem—beautiful, adventurous, and full of natural surprises. The scenery is stunning with dramatic rock formations, long stretches of sand, and that wild, rugged coastline that’s perfect for exploring.

gerickes-point.author: Stopbiki .com
gerickes-point.text: 🌊 Gericke’s Point, Swartvlei | StopBiki Beautiful coastal spot with clear tide pools full of sea life at low tide. Small fish, sea stars and anemones make it perfect for photos and family exploring. A peaceful Garden Route must-see. ✔️

gerickes-point.author: Nicolene Fourie
gerickes-point.text: Gericke’s Point in Sedgefield is truly one of the most beautiful spots in South Africa. The combination of golden beaches, dramatic rock formations, and crystal-clear ocean views makes it feel almost untouched. It’s perfect for a peaceful walk, a swim, or just sitting and watching the waves crash against the rocks.

tweede-tol.author: Tatum Arendse
tweede-tol.text: Would recommend 👌🏻 Thank you to the Neli at the gate for being so welcoming🙏🏻 The private campsite has its own bathroom with shower and toilet (shower pressure isn't the greatest).

tweede-tol.author: Natalie Wessels
tweede-tol.text: Hiked from Tweedetol to the secret waterfall. Rock hopped all the way back to Tweedetol. Stopped at some amazing pools along the way. An amazing experience.

tweede-tol.author: Trinity Non-Ferrous Metal
tweede-tol.text: Nice and quiet place really enjoyed relaxing there not very busy. We did the little hike it was beautiful. There are lots of baboons around some that come into the campsite but they run if you shout at them.

st-blaize-hiking-trail.author: Trudie Pieterse
st-blaize-hiking-trail.text: Breathtaking views along the coastline. It is supposed to be a moderate hike, but we got lost a few times due to unclear/no signage. But still worth spending the 6 hours in the most beautiful environment and appreciating nature. Make sure to start early in summer.

st-blaize-hiking-trail.author: Eben Van der Merwe
st-blaize-hiking-trail.text: It’s a wonderful hike with breathtaking views, just be prepared for some rocky terrain towards Dana Bay’s side of the hike, and take plenty of water along; there is a tap to refill water at the entry to Pinnacle Point golf estate(from Mosselbay’s side 6km into the hike), through which the trail passes.

st-blaize-hiking-trail.author: Anze Webster
st-blaize-hiking-trail.text: An amazing hiking trail with spectacular views of the wild ocean. It is a 13.5km hike between Mossel Bay point to Dana Bay which takes around 5 hours to complete. There are some climbing and uphill areas with a total elevation gain of 425 meters. The trail was mostly well marked.

sanparks-woodville-big-tree.author: Saralee
sanparks-woodville-big-tree.text: A lovely, easy 2km hike through the forest with an abundance of birds, plants, mushrooms.... Was beautiful and peaceful (except we were told how to warn off a troop of baboons should we come head to head with them!!) Highly recommended.

sanparks-woodville-big-tree.author: corne kruger
sanparks-woodville-big-tree.text: You just have to see it for your self. Fotos are helpful but. Seeing it in person is the best. We always pack a picnic basket and bake pancakes.

sanparks-woodville-big-tree.author: Ilaria Semprini-Sara
sanparks-woodville-big-tree.text: Loved this hike - even the uphill of the 7km loop. So tranquil, we even disturbed the resident baboons during their early morning shenanigans! Highly recommend this magical hike!

devils-peak-hike-start.author: Nicole H
devils-peak-hike-start.text: Definitely go on this hike. Not difficult at all. Had a vwey young family with young kids.

devils-peak-hike-start.author: Enid Son
devils-peak-hike-start.text: We did this beautiful hike just after the rains to see the waterfalls. We did not hike up to Devils Peak - we walked the lower contour path. Beautiful scenery of Table Mountain and the city below. The path is well maintained. A few big rocks to climb and some little streams but all very doable and safe .

devils-peak-hike-start.author: Jalize Uys
devils-peak-hike-start.text: Hiked to Devil's Peak from Rhodes Memorial, via Mowbray Ridge and Knife's Edge. Went down the front of Table Mountain (we went with two cars). Don't do this hike if you are unfit and/or struggle with hights!

whale-tail-lookout-point.author: Brian Paine
whale-tail-lookout-point.text: Lovely lookout point, even the shark spotting lady was sitting there. Slight sewage smell coming from a drain nearby but you can only smell it when the wind blows in your direction..

whale-tail-lookout-point.author: Travel diary
whale-tail-lookout-point.text: Nice view...but I must say big beach so can get crowded during holidays...nice to go early or just take pic and then move t⁸o less crowded beaches as there are many at plettenberg.

whale-tail-lookout-point.author: Gerhard Geldenhuys
whale-tail-lookout-point.text: A great viewpoint and photography spot when you're in Plettenburg bay. A interesting thing, there was a lady sitting by the tail with binoculars and making notes. I asked her what she was observing or studying. The answer... She is a shark spotter! And if there's any in the vicinity, she radios in to clear the swimmers.

newlands-forest-hiking-trail.author: vernon singh
newlands-forest-hiking-trail.text: Hiking in Newlands Forest is a calming, comfortable, and safe experience. You can walk, hike, run, or picnic at your leisure. If you’re looking for a challenge, look no further Skeleton Gorge and Nursery Ravine are great choices and will definitely get your heart rate up.

newlands-forest-hiking-trail.author: Jaco van Zyl
newlands-forest-hiking-trail.text: Awesome walking/hiking trail with free parking and no entrance fees. Peaceful and calm, great to have such magnificent nature scenes in the city. Great coffee 'van' at the finish/start with very friendly owner and staff. Kids and dogs friendly, safe trail with easy to moderate paths. Perfect for family or friends outing.

newlands-forest-hiking-trail.author: rachel fiskus
newlands-forest-hiking-trail.text: Newlands Forest is absolutely beautiful easily one of my favorite places in Cape Town. The views are incredible, and the whole area is full of lush South African greenery, huge trees, ferns, and endless natural beauty. There are even caves you can explore that open up to astonishing viewpoints.

marloth-nature-reserve.author: Ann Price
marloth-nature-reserve.text: A impulse visit after we had driven to bontebok park which was closed due to flood damage. We were amazed at its beauty, mountain views, clean pretty picnic spot & walking trails. We attempted the waterfall trail from the picnic site, but it became quite testing so we turned around. Well worth a visit.

marloth-nature-reserve.author: Benita Kritzinger
marloth-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful scenery indeed! Just be aware that "moderate", as indicated on the route map, is not quite as moderate (coming from a avid trail runner) ...and tripple check your route as the markers are also not very good and can become very confusing at certain spots.

marloth-nature-reserve.author: Erlank van der Merwe
marloth-nature-reserve.text: Absolutely stunning hike! Tough but very enjoyable and rewarding. Trail is well marked with clean and comfortable huts. Big thank you to Gregory and the friendly staff at Marloth Reserve who went out of their way to make sure we were well informed and safe to enjoy this wonderful hike! Well done Cape Nature, see you soon!

diepwalle-forest.author: Martin Stevens
diepwalle-forest.text: Diepwalle Forest and the Prince Alfred Pass offer one of the most scenic and rewarding drives in the Garden Route, especially for those who appreciate history, nature, and quiet surroundings.

diepwalle-forest.author: Kevin Harris
diepwalle-forest.text: Authentic knysna forest experience. Three great forest trails and a quaint camp site. To get there, you need to drive through a piece of the township, but it's been fine each time we've driven through.

diepwalle-forest.author: Carl
diepwalle-forest.text: We recently stayed at the SANParks Knysna Tented Camps for our Anniversary and the natural setting is without a doubt the standout feature. The camp is surrounded by rich indigenous forest, with enormous trees and beautiful trails that make every walk feel special.

chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.author: Ellen Coetzee
chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.text: What a climb!!!! Not easy, but doable. Wear hiking boots to support ankles and take a walking stick for going down. Backpack with enough (a lot) water, snacks and a light jacket. Absolutely beautiful with clear path all the way to the top.

chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.author: Colleen Mclintock
chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.text: This is not a beginners hike or for young children, we saw both and they were not happy. It's majestic and very steep. We started at 730 but it was already very hot, suggest you go early. Busy trail, the top was very full with people taking selfies out on the ledge, that was scary, also ruined the vibe. We did go in peak holiday season.

chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.author: Vaughn B
chapmans-peak-hiking-trail-start-point.text: This is probably my favorite hike at the moment. Not too popular with gorgeous views.

kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.author: Tawanda Irvine Makoni
kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.text: The hike is fairly easy and suitable even for beginners. It’s not too challenging overall, and the views at the waterfalls are worth it. There are two waterfalls — reaching the second one can be a bit tricky for anyone who’s not comfortable climbing with the help of chains.

kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.author: Bryn Barker
kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.text: The Elandsrivier hike is an easy recommendation. 1.5 to 2 hours each way depending on your pace, leans more beginner difficulty than intermediate, the scenery is beautiful, the trail is reasonably well maintained, very kid friendly and there are several decent lunch and swimming spots.

kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.author: Asmaa Akoon
kromrivier-and-elandsrivier-hiking-trail.text: this hike is beautiful, the waterfall is amazing- we spent almost 3 hours up there, the second chains were a bit tough and required upper body strength. the way back felt like it took forever and it was so hot, sunscreen and hats are a must! waterproof hiking shoes are also recommended as you walk through the river

greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.author: lotte peniel
greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.text: What a beautiful place to walk in. It's for free to get in! And its absolutely stunning with a waterfall running into a river. It's worth a visit.

greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.author: Johan Human
greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.text: This is truly a great experience! You can enjoy nature at its best. The trail routes are clearly marked. We did not have any difficulties navigating along the well kept trails. The nature reserve staf are friendly and helpful. Take your swimming gear with when you do the waterfall route.

greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.author: Nic Shaw
greyton-nature-reserve-start-of-boesmans.text: Greyton Nature Reserve is simply beautiful and well worth a walk. Like any mountain environment, make sure you have water, some warm clothing, a map or app, and don’t walk alone #IamWildernessSafe

otter-trail-start.author: viren javiya
otter-trail-start.text: Starting point of one of the most famous hiking trails in South Africa. The surroundings are absolutely stunning, with ocean views, cliffs, and dense forest all around. Even if you’re not doing the full trail, visiting this spot gives you a great feel of the natural beauty of Tsitsikamma.

otter-trail-start.author: Barefoot mart
otter-trail-start.text: Great challenging trek. We lost the trail markings and clambered over the rocks lo ger then needed but all was good. Watch your step but watch for yellow trail markings too. Not much water from the falls, (dry spell) still amazing. It took us 3+ hours with lunch and photos.

otter-trail-start.author: Adele Duvenage
otter-trail-start.text: The Otter Trail Start in Tsitsikamma is the gateway to one of South Africa’s most legendary hiking adventures! Even if you’re just visiting the starting point, the scenery is absolutely breathtaking — rugged coastline, lush forest, and the sound of waves crashing against the rocks.

jubilee-creek-picnic-site.author: Nicolene Platjies
jubilee-creek-picnic-site.text: Jubilee Creek Trail and Picnic Site: A Hidden Gem Nestled away from urban chaos, Jubilee Creek offers a delightful escape into nature's embrace. This enchanting location combines scenic hiking trails with well-appointed picnic facilities, making it perfect for families and nature enthusiasts alike.

jubilee-creek-picnic-site.author: Justine Dreyer
jubilee-creek-picnic-site.text: Such a gem. Picnic area clean and shaded along the river, mowed grass, braai areas, bathrooms have toilet paper. Quiet... sounds of nature and water, and peace. We have been there many times and along the walking trail I have seen chameleons and boomslang and knysna loeries and some really interesting frogs and insects.

jubilee-creek-picnic-site.author: Adam Bentley
jubilee-creek-picnic-site.text: Lovely easy walk up to the waterfall 3.6km total. Well shaded, make sure you a buy SanParks permit for the walk. At the start of the walk lovely picnic areas with braai facilities.

constantia-nek-hiking-trail.author: Liann Koen
constantia-nek-hiking-trail.text: Really lovely piece of mountain. We started at Constantia Nek and made our way to Skeleton Gorge. My only complaint would be the dog walkers who don't clean up after their animals. It creates quite a stench at the start of the trail which is very disappointing. The scenery is lovely and there a lovely shaded parts along the route

constantia-nek-hiking-trail.author: Ike Moriz
constantia-nek-hiking-trail.text: I have so many amazing memories of hiking up this trail with friends and family over the decades!! The Constantia Nek hiking trail is absolutely beautiful — one of my favourite hikes in the world! The route follows a scenic jeep track all the way to the top, where you’re rewarded with a lovely dam and plenty of birdlife along the way.

constantia-nek-hiking-trail.author: DEMETRIUS BAILEY
constantia-nek-hiking-trail.text: A wonderful trail for moderate to hard. You can choose to go up and loop back down or to go right across nursery ravine or skeleton gorge and back around that way. Great if you have a lot of time to spend.

kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.author: Duncan Alfreds
kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.text: Kasteelspoort Hiking trail is one of the Table Mountain legends as it’s been around for decades and been walked by generations – sometimes of the same family. It’s a rewarding trail that takes you up Table Mountain and gives the opportunity to navigate to one of the many huts, the dams of even all the way up to the Table Mountain Cable Wa…

kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.author: O.O A
kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.text: Not a hard hike… We was up and down within 3.5hrs… Kudos to the group that tagged me along @Omegaascenthikers.

kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.author: Rick R
kasteelspoort-hiking-trail.text: One of the many ways up Table mountain from the pipe track. You can reach the "diving board" via this route. Not too difficult but long uphill sections and strenuous. The Upper Cable station is 2 hours walk from the top of this track and involves multiple uphills even after reaching the top. So you must be fit to attempt this route.

woodstock-cave.author: vernon singh
woodstock-cave.text: The contour path running parallel to offers an incredible waterfall hike, passing five waterfalls along the way, with as an added bonus. It’s the perfect stop for lunch, a rest, and unforgettable photos overlooking the CBD, the bay, harbour, and .

woodstock-cave.author: Elizabeth O'Rourke
woodstock-cave.text: Did this easy 1.15 min hike up with 2 boys under 12(altogether 5km) We started from Rhodes Memorial. There isnt much signage or direction to the path but I downloaded the map and everyone we passed confirmed the way. Easy walk for beginners and family.

woodstock-cave.author: Arne Roux
woodstock-cave.text: It's an easy stroll if you're moderately fit. Great for a beginners hike. Beautiful views from this massive cave. Only downside is, homeless folk leave pollution behind.

steenbras-river-gorge.author: Taahir Dawood
steenbras-river-gorge.text: Crystal Pools is an absolute thrill of a hike. We booked in advance to ensure everyone was sorted and we made sure to hike right until the last dam. There is a massive incline before the final spot, but it was well worth it. We had fantastic weather, a great group and overall, a beautiful experience.

steenbras-river-gorge.author: Mike & Brittnie Little
steenbras-river-gorge.text: Steenbras River Gorge is a breathtaking natural spot in the Western Cape. The river winds through dramatic cliffs and lush vegetation, creating stunning scenery. Perfect for hiking, photography, or just enjoying the peaceful outdoors. The trails are well marked, and the area feels remote and unspoiled.

steenbras-river-gorge.author: Andre Hackart
steenbras-river-gorge.text: We did the Crystal Pools hike on 30 Nov. It was certainly worth waiting. Opening times were confusing though as when I contacted the reserve they said opening 6.30am yet when we arrived the staff on duty said opening at 7am. The trail itself is well marked in most places with feet on the rocks or red tipped poles.

india-venster-hiking-trail.author: Mahmut Batır
india-venster-hiking-trail.text: I recently completed the India Venster hiking trail, and it was undoubtedly the most exhausting hike I've ever done. This final stage is designed for hikers ready to challenge themselves without specialized climbing equipment. The views are absolutely stunning — truly breathtaking.

india-venster-hiking-trail.author: Stephan Kehry
india-venster-hiking-trail.text: Amazing trail. No doubt. But to all those who have a rather pleasant picture about what is laying in front of you: Don't underestimate the technical demands and requirements during the hike. Having been bouldering before surely helps. And don't let you talk into it. Finally it surely is an extremely dangerous tour.

india-venster-hiking-trail.author: Lucy
india-venster-hiking-trail.text: It was a great hike with stunning views! There are some difficult sections and if you're afraid of heights this is NOT for you... I wouldn't say I'm an experienced hiker but with a good overall fitness it's doable. But I have to admit there were some pretty scary parts.

natures-valley-beach.author: Jahn Hingst
natures-valley-beach.text: Title: Nature's Valley Beach: One of the World's Most Special Untouched Gems! (Late review from our recent Garden Route trip) Review: Nature's Valley Beach has to rank in the top 35 (if not higher!) of the world's best beaches—it's that pristine, raw, and soul-stirring.

natures-valley-beach.author: Travel diary
natures-valley-beach.text: We went on this beach while starting beach side of salt river hikes...(only try if it is low tide, no rains and if you are fit and in proper hiking gears like shoes, waterproof jacket etc especially with kids-we couldnt finish due to rains as it seems to becoming a bit tricky and slipery so came back after point 5) ...for my son this beac…

natures-valley-beach.author: Rajesh Yandrathi
natures-valley-beach.text: Wow!! What an absolutely stunning experience! The lagoon, beach, and sea were all breathtaking—everything from the crystal-clear, turquoise waters to the dramatic cliffs surrounding the area. The sea was calm, and the water was warm and inviting, perfect for a refreshing dip or a leisurely walk along the shore.

silvermine-waterfall.author: Adam Bawden
silvermine-waterfall.text: Really easy to hike, great views, both of the waterfall and the mountains. Suits people of all ages. You can bring your dogs. Just beware of the rocks going down to the actual waterfall, around 1.4km into the hike. The rocks are slippery because of sand on the rocks, and sand on your shoes. The hike tracked 3km on my watch.

silvermine-waterfall.author: Duncan Alfreds
silvermine-waterfall.text: This little hike can be anything from a 20-minute refresh to 4 hours if you're feeling energetic. It starts as easy as you like and in about 20 minutes, you can view the waterfall. Free tip: Take the trail down slowly - it can be slippery in wet weather. There's lots of opportunity for great pics and soaking up the sun is also fantastic.

silvermine-waterfall.author: Nadine Carroll
silvermine-waterfall.text: Spending time in nature makes me happy! We enjoyed a mindful stroll to the top of Silvermine Waterfall with sunshine, birdsong and beautiful views along the way. We'll tackle the steep steps down to the waterfall next time. The Proteas were just beginning to bloom, so we'll return soon to see them as there are hundreds of Protea bushes.

half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.author: Stephan Henrico
half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.text: This is the 2nd time I did this trail. The 1st was canoeing from Fairy Knowe Hotel then climbing the last 2 or so km to the waterfall. Today we did the full monty. Excellent hike and saw no less than 3 Knysna Loeries. Amzing! Amazing! Amazing!

half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.author: JShannon
half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.text: Visited today. Green trail is easier & takes around 2 hrs to the waterfall. Kids loved the pontoon & the little swimming spot about 500m from there. It was a welcome stop before a fairly steep climb to the waterfall. The waterfall is beautiful but mainly boulders, with few shady spots. Water is gorgeous!

half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.author: Amsa
half-collared-kingfisher-trail-waterfall.text: A very easy stroll with amazing views - we saw a very tamed bushbuck that did not mind us at all - view at the top so rewarding! They don’t take payment at the gate - they take payment at the parking lot across the bridge. Took us 2hours to walk - 8km We were rushing because of time - we entered at 2pm - gate close at 6

elsies-peak.author: Nicolas Lorenzo
elsies-peak.text: Elsie’s Peak gives breathtaking views of both the Fish Hoek and Glencairn valleys as well as False Bay. The hike up is steep in some sections and gets rocky here and there but is fairly easy. Lots of fynbos plants and animal life in the area so please leave no trace.

elsies-peak.author: Hishaam
elsies-peak.text: Great beginners hike, even older kids could do it. Only complaints was that it was a bit windy at the top and there's no dedicated parking

elsies-peak.author: Barry Coltham
elsies-peak.text: Windy and exhilarating with wonderful views of the Deep South

danie-miller-hiking-trail.author: IZYCRAFTS
danie-miller-hiking-trail.text: Was a really beautiful trail.. lots of rocks and pebbles. If u haven't hiked for a while this is bit rough.. we'll for.me though because I haven't even walked recently and climbing over rocks was a little hectic. I met older people than me and the didnt even hit a sweat. So practice makes perfect..

danie-miller-hiking-trail.author: Cobus Strauss
danie-miller-hiking-trail.text: The hiking path is very rocky and there isn’t really a proper walkway. The views are amazing, but you still have some houses very close to the route in your perfect photo's way.

danie-miller-hiking-trail.author: Trystan William Gale
danie-miller-hiking-trail.text: A really easy hike. Steep in the beginning, but with a mild gradient and varied terrain. Turning around at the GB anchor was perfect. A great walk for a young dog getting into trails. At the right time of year, there are a few streams for your furry friend to drink from.

walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.author: Wynand Nel
walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.text: Walker Bay Nature Reserve is a wonderful destination for nature lovers. Visitors can explore the famous Klipgat Caves, relax on the beach, enjoy scenic walking trails, make use of fishing spots, and even tackle the 4x4 trail.

walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.author: Martin Garland
walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.text: We visited Walker Bay Nature Reserve on Saturday, 9th May 2026 and were disgusted with the behavior of fishermen and 4x4 drivers. There were cars parked all over certain beaches to the point that we could not walk along the beach. We saw cars zig zagging along the beach, going up through the dunes again and back onto the next beach.

walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.author: Tatum Radloff
walker-bay-nature-reserve-entrance.text: A 5 star experience in regards to the place itself. It has the bluest water and Klipgat caves was amazing to see. The beauty reminds me of images of an island holiday. Unfortunately, when we went, there was a rude and unfriendly lady who dealt with myself and husband quitre abruptly.

banhoek-conservancy.author: Val Waddle
banhoek-conservancy.text: Beautiful walk along the side of the mountain. Mainly for bike trails but it also is a nice hike on multiple trails. Be sure to bring water. On a weekday we had the park to ourselves.

banhoek-conservancy.author: Nicole Wahl
banhoek-conservancy.text: I really enjoyed the hike to Duiwelskloof. The views were stunning. Not an easy hike, but also not too difficult, depending on your fitness level and fear of heights and rocks. There are lots of rock scrambling. And a lot of cairns to help guide the way.

banhoek-conservancy.author: Rick R
banhoek-conservancy.text: This is a beautiful conservancy which is private but open to the public for free. There are hiking and mountain viking trails. We hiked up to Banhoek Waterfall which is a small waterfall with a pool hidden in the mountain - a stunning place. The area is safe and there is enough parking available inside the gate.

leopards-kloof-waterfall.author: Alec Munnik
leopards-kloof-waterfall.text: Such a beautiful experience and easy hike, especially if you start via the gardens.

leopards-kloof-waterfall.author: TG Hall
leopards-kloof-waterfall.text: A fantastic hike starting from Harold Porter and winding up through Leopard Gorge. It’s not a particularly strenuous hike but be warned the walk requires lots of boulder hopping and ladder climbing which was difficult for the seniors in our party and especially when wet!

leopards-kloof-waterfall.author: Iviwe
leopards-kloof-waterfall.text: Absolutely gorgeous views to the top and at the top as well. It's an easy hike to the waterfall. Reception claims it's a 2H hike. 1H up, 1H down, but my friend and I did it in 1H 20min at a moderate pace, but it is a must do. It's a moderate level climb, and it's so much fun with not too much energy expenditure. I am not an expert hiker.

kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.author: Bethel Selfcatering
kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.text: We had such a wonderful experience hiking the Circles in the Forest trail. The route is absolutely beautiful and peaceful, surrounded by tall ancient trees and breathtaking forest scenery.

kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.author: Marike Meiring
kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.text: If you've been to Knysna and not yet walked 'Kringe In die Bos', my friend you're missing out big time. Famous for the Dalene Matthee memorial tree, a special place this well known South African author visited often and where her beautiful novels of Fiela se Kind en Kringe in die bos where born.

kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.author: Sandra Potgieter
kringe-in-n-bos-hiking-trail.text: One of the most beautiful hiking trails we have done. Before you start there is a sign in office of SANParks. R61 pp. short drive from there to get to the trail. The trail itself starts at the Daleen Matthee memorial.

gamkaberg-nature-reserve.author: Eduard de Koning
gamkaberg-nature-reserve.text: A fantastic reserve with awesome views. The Lawson's Pass 4x4 is a must do. The Eco lodge accommodation is very well planned and perfect comfort and luxury in this succulent karoo reserve.

gamkaberg-nature-reserve.author: A C
gamkaberg-nature-reserve.text: Stayed for a few days with family at Tierskloof eco-lodge. Exceptional campsite and views. Only let down (which turned out to be major), was the baboons. There were literally about 25 surrounding the camp and they would come into our camp if we were at the pool/in our tents.

gamkaberg-nature-reserve.author: Vanessa Raath
gamkaberg-nature-reserve.text: Had an awesome 3 night stay at the Xami Eco-lodge which was very quiet and beautiful! We were there in September and the flowers were amazing. We went up the 4x4 Lawsons Pass which was great but long, I would recommend turning back at the viewpoint if you don't have all day to get out the other side of the park...

skeleton-gorge-waterfall.author: Chloé Schwizgebel
skeleton-gorge-waterfall.text: A fantastic hike to the cable car, starting in the Kirstenbosch botanical garden. Beware that the elevation is big (1080m) and the top much colder than at the bottom. But sure to be equipped with an extra warmer top and enough water as the hike is done in at least 3h (up to 5 easily)

skeleton-gorge-waterfall.author: Simone Daniels
skeleton-gorge-waterfall.text: The trail is very steep and you definitely can feel it in your knees. Comfortable and proper hiking shoes needed the climb the rocky steps and wooden ladders. Trail has a lot of shade, which makes it easier to do. Views is so rewarding.

skeleton-gorge-waterfall.author: Christina
skeleton-gorge-waterfall.text: I hiked up the skeleton George waterfall and then down the nursery ravine (overall a 3 hour loop, there is a map on Komoot for example). Very nice trail. There is a ladder on the way up, but not exposed, so quite safe.

phillipskop-mountain-reserve.author: Rob Jones
phillipskop-mountain-reserve.text: Such a spectacular mountain reserve. The accommodation is spacious, well equipped and have amazing views. The natural pool is beautiful. The hiking trails are well signposted and the paths are clear. I went on a trail run and the trails are great for running on too.

phillipskop-mountain-reserve.author: Antoinette
phillipskop-mountain-reserve.text: It didn't take me long to realise the ecolodge style & setting resembles my dream home. Spacious, comfortable, nestled into nature, and offering the perfect environment for resetting the nervous system into deep relaxation mode.

phillipskop-mountain-reserve.author: David Gertzen
phillipskop-mountain-reserve.text: An excellent place for a self catering weekend away in the mountains with friends or family. 2 hours from Cape Town. You can even have families in adjacent houses. The four guest houses have magnificent views, are spacious and well equipped.

brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.author: Kay Claire
brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.text: Great walk, very scenic and a lot to look at. We took 2 and a half hours to do the walk because we were enjoying looking at plants along the way. The information about the walk said it crosses the water 5 times, but I counted 9 water crossings.

brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.author: Elizabeth Gomes
brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.text: Beautiful Trail! Lush, with abundance of trees and river crossings. Bird life and lots to look at, so the distance of 4km took us a little longer than expected. 😍

brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.author: Lauren Greasley
brownhooded-kingfisher-trail.text: Completed in early summer. Suggest early morning, first thing. Easy trail, well maintained , though crossing the river at several points are across rocks. Dogs are not allowed.

kranspad-staproete.author: David Apter
kranspad-staproete.text: The Marine hotel pool on the coastal path is magnificent and alive with marine life. Visit in late pm and experience looking for marine life with ultraviolet light. An incredible experience for both adults and children. Brilliant place for a family picnic.

kranspad-staproete.author: Tanja De Lange
kranspad-staproete.text: Fantastic walk along the cliffs with many lookout points to spot whales just off the shore. Clean, safe and pet friendly. The path takes you all along the coastline and oasses through the main centre of Hermanus town where you can find restaurants, shops and many other places

kranspad-staproete.author: Duncan Alfreds
kranspad-staproete.text: What an enjoyable walk through what some may describe as the heart of Hermanus. This is where you will find the most tourist traffic and it's busy - even when the rest of the town begins to bed down for the night.

murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.author: Sylvanna Wilson
murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.text: Murludi is a working farm with the most beautiful views of the valley. If you are like me and enjoy seeing the operational side of things, the farmers take you on a private tour of their fruit drying operation for only R150pp this was a once in a lifetime experience! I bought most of their items in the fridge and WHAT A TREAT!!!

murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.author: Ms D
murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.text: Lovely 8km hike, with a shorter loop option and a dam you can swim in en route. The pack of dried fruit that comes with the permit was divine - some of the best fried fruit I've had 😋

murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.author: Bibi Rasti Adamgee
murludi-hiking-trail-and-dried-fruit.text: If you would like to do the tour, call and book in advance to ensure the person responsible for the tour is available. We took a stroll through the hydrangea garden as we did not book in advance. Was worth the visit and be sure to keep your eyes peeled for a small signage poster as maps does not pick it up easily.

second-waterfall.author: Mahier Dawood
second-waterfall.text: The final stretch of the hike to the second waterfall is the most beautiful and rewarding experience. An absolute gem of nature. The nature reserved only allows a limited number of cars in per day so you need to be there early in order to drive in the start of the hike, or come a bit later after cars come out, or walk from the entrance ga…

second-waterfall.author: Zoie Does Things
second-waterfall.text: Did this hike a week back and it was great. Just a few tips : -Walking from the entrance gate will add 10km to the hike, otherwise you can drive in. -The earlier the better as parking inside can run out. -The hike is pretty mild except for one uphill stretch near the waterfall.

second-waterfall.author: Bernhard Renner
second-waterfall.text: Great hike. Well worth the effort. From the gate it's around a 7.5km hike to the top. Some steep sections on the single track, some areas can definitely be seen as dangerous. Couple of nice swimming spots along the way to the top. Round trip back to gate gets you a total of just under 16km. Hidden gem of Stellenbosch.

cape-columbine-nature-reserve.author: JR
cape-columbine-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful West Coast (Cap Columbine), s must visit. Remember cash gor entrance fee.

cape-columbine-nature-reserve.author: Larochelle Van der Vyver
cape-columbine-nature-reserve.text: Cape Columbine Nature Reserve is a beautiful place to visit – the drive in and around the park is lovely, and the coastline with its wildflowers in season is definitely the highlight. Unfortunately, the card machine at the gate wasn’t working when we arrived, which was a bit inconvenient.

cape-columbine-nature-reserve.author: Riza Arendse
cape-columbine-nature-reserve.text: I've been coming here since 4 months old. Both my sons have been camping since they were babies. If it's peace and tranquility you're looking for, this is it. The ocean will always feed you and the community of Paternoster is warm and welcoming. Family time is fun when camping. I'm looking forward to coming here another 43 years.

plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.author: jax giles
plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.text: A magical few days in a beautiful part of our country (first time for me!) - best way to see Plett through different eyes. A comfortable hike through amazing scenery, different trails and tracks, super comfy accommodation every night and just waaaay too much yummy home cooked food along the way too.

plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.author: Bernard Donnelly
plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.text: Wonderful week exploring the unseen side of Plett walking through the varied biomes in the hills and valleys behind Plettenberg Bay. Outstanding guides and support crew, high standard accommodation and food and wonderful treats and surprises on the way.

plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.author: Deborah Grafton-Rosewell
plett-camino-hiking-trail-slackpacking.text: My first Camino and the first of many!!! The build up to this Camino was major for me, being a stay@home mom i had to prepare, plan and put many many plans into action. By the time the day arrived to my departure I was depleted of all emotional, physical energy, arriving at our first accomodation in Plett was just what I needed, out of th…

ceres-mountain-fynbos-nature-reserve.author: Suzanne Olwagen
ceres-mountain-fynbos-nature-reserve.text: Beautiful,tranquil and with breathtaking views. I would definitely recommend you to put this place on your bucket list. The only let down was the last 20 minutes drive on a not so good dirt road. Maybe it was because of the heavy rains, but take this into account when you travel. Maybe a bakkie would be a better option.

ceres-mountain-fynbos-nature-reserve.author: Roxy Theunissen
ceres-mountain-fynbos-nature-reserve.text: Absolutely beautiful place! Breathtaking views & tranquil surroundings!!

drie-damme-hiking-trail.author: Daniel Molander
drie-damme-hiking-trail.text: We had a pleasant hike up to the first dam for a swim and then hiked back. There are several route options; the one we chose was 2,5km one way and quite tough and required some light climbing.

drie-damme-hiking-trail.author: Dee McCrindle
drie-damme-hiking-trail.text: Absolutely stunning experience! A few route options to get there. Very relaxed flat path until the last bit. Lots of spots at the dam where the whole family can enjoy. Very refreshing swim, with great views.

drie-damme-hiking-trail.author: Heinrich Odendaal
drie-damme-hiking-trail.text: We did an amazing 5km walk/climb from Voelklip to Drie Damme it took us just under 3 hours. Spectacular views with an easy walk until the first dam from there it starts getting tricky but still possible for beginners. The trail is easily visible till the 3rd dam no way of getting lost. Highly recommend going the extra mile to the 3rd dam.

wild-wellness.author: Estelle van Niekerk
wild-wellness.text: We were fortunate to have a very nice group of people which I believe is a big contributing factor in making it an unforgettable experience. The scenery was amazing. Our accommodation was excellent. Tips to improve: I feel the menu could be better for the price of the whole hike. The impression was created that lunch will be included.

wild-wellness.author: Karen Steyn
wild-wellness.text: Walking with Whales was absolutely beautiful in every way. Nature, flowers, whales, accommodation, food - everything exceeded my expectation. The guides where so helpful and considerate in every way. The knowledge that they have about nature and the surroundings that we were, made it an absolute amazing hike and adventure.

wild-wellness.author: Cathy Cleary
wild-wellness.text: An unforgettable 5 days doing the Whale Walk with Wild Wellness. Our guides, Simone and Jono, were so knowledgeable, thoughtful and caring. The accommodation and meals were superb. And the highlight of seeing whales in numbers and close proximity exceeded my expectations.

perlemoen-trail.author: Yuliya MD
perlemoen-trail.text: Went on this trip in November with a bunch of friends, and it was honestly one of the highlights of my year. Our guides, Jason and Billy, were exceptional, warm, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about the natural beauty of the Western Cape. They showed us hidden spots and shared stories that made every stop feel meaningful.

perlemoen-trail.author: Maryke Henton
perlemoen-trail.text: We hiked it from 5th to the 9th of May, 2026. It was a wonderful experience. I thought I was knowledgeable about South Africa's beaches and dunes, but was proved wrong, by the very competent guides, Jason and Billy, who enriched each day by showing and telling us all about the marine life, dune vegetation, history of the area, and best of…

perlemoen-trail.author: Renu Lakha
perlemoen-trail.text: Did this trip over the Easter weekend with friends. WHAT A BLAST. A big shout out to Our amazing guides Jason and Billy who made our experience most memorable with their incredible knowledge and passion For preservation of the most beautiful unspoiled beaches in the Cape. Definitely a bucket list item.❣️👌

sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.author: Peter Lourens
sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.text: A great hking trail, with moderate fitness required. The 3 trails are well marked and neatly maintained. It takes you through lush green indigenous forests. The mountain streams had cool fresh water despite the drought in the Garden Route

sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.author: corne kruger
sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.text: We started walking around 10 am. Decided to walk the red trail. What a beautiful trail indeed. On the map it shows a place to 🏊‍♂️..... but we thought off taking a detour to go to the famous ( infinity pools ) for a swim. And a picnic on the rocks. The trail is going to be a challenge, but don't give up. It's all worth it in the end.

sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.author: Sarita Friguglietti
sanparks-groeneweide-hiking-trail.text: Did the green trail with 10 and 12 year old. Easy hike, nothing too steep, under foliage most of the time. The start was tricky to find. Drive thru university entrance gates (we were asked to sign in), follow the road as it curves right, pass 2 tennis courts and cricket oval. Park just past the first big, white hostel.

vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.author: Chad Van Zyl
vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.text: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ I had an amazing stay at Vrolijkheid Nature Reserve for my birthday! The accommodation exceeded all expectations — the recent upgrades to the units really make a difference. The rooms were spotless, stylish, and super comfortable.

vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.author: Shurine van Niekerk
vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.text: It was a lovely experience. Cottages are beautiful and clean and there are wi-fi connection sporadically.

vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.author: Chantel Pienaar
vrolijkheid-nature-reserve.text: Stayed over the weekend of 1 March 2024. Thank you to friendly Aphiwe at the bookings office who assisted with a smile and some great advice. Fantastic value accommodation at Vrolijkheid Nature Reserve. With only 5 x self catering units, this reserve really offers so much.

the-fynbos-trail.author: Sonja Niedermann
the-fynbos-trail.text: We thoroughly enjoyed this beautiful 3 day hike through the Fynbos. We booked the self guided budget option which was a great choice. Our bags and food were transported from hut to hut and everything was packed away into the fridges so that we could have a cold beer and a glass of wine on arrival at the hut.

the-fynbos-trail.author: Andre Zietsman
the-fynbos-trail.text: There were 8 of us including our guide Tim Lundry. We had an absolutely wonderful time. The views, the flowers, the scenery was EPIC! Our accommodations were wonderful. I am not fit, do not exercise and I managed well. Had a few blisters on the 3rd day going back to our cars.

the-fynbos-trail.author: Sasha Campbell
the-fynbos-trail.text: We have done many different hikes around South Africa, the Fynbos Trail is definitely one of my tops hikes!!!! Our guide Christoff was simply amazing and so much fun. He hand led 15 of us ladies so well and landed up being such a big part of our weekend. His knowledge and passion was next level.

witfontein-nature-reserve.author: Gavin B
witfontein-nature-reserve.text: Most beautiful and peaceful pass to cross as a family outing. The old Montagu Pass sand road is eroded due to flooding and a 2x4 or 4x4 vehicle is recommended.

witfontein-nature-reserve.author: sacha coull
witfontein-nature-reserve.text: A beautiful breathtaking walk. If you follow the path along the cradock pathway, you can have a swim in the river down below.

witfontein-nature-reserve.author: Bennie Gerber
witfontein-nature-reserve.text: Took a bike trip through there and it was just amazing and awesome

old-george-dam.author: ELROY KENSLEY
old-george-dam.text: Beautifull hike not to long and not difficult

old-george-dam.author: Eugene Dippenaar
old-george-dam.text: Nice little walk to the old dam wall. A little tricky to find on Google maps if you not a local but got there eventually

helderberg-disa-kloof.author: Zandre thesituation Leonards
helderberg-disa-kloof.text: What a nice trails it’s a pity it only open at 07:30 but the trail is amazing love the west peak and Dome trail somewhat challenging. The Top is amazing love the view of strand and Sir Lowry Pass. Safe for children will definitely go again.

helderberg-disa-kloof.author: Sixolile Mabombo-Benson
helderberg-disa-kloof.text: Awesome space, good amenities and lovely picnic areas at the base and at junctions on the trails. The trails are well-marked and the views are fantastic. The Protea trail is a nice moderate walk for beginners to intermediate hikers.

helderberg-disa-kloof.author: Gonzalo Linares
helderberg-disa-kloof.text: We go to hike there or to climb the mountains, after the recent fire they have made a new hiking trail that goes directly to the gorge. This is the first new trail in years, it is a single track so gets a bit busy especially now. The plant life is coming in nicely and it is well worth it to take in the nature or for some exercise.

duiwelsbos-waterfall.author: Luschka van Onselen
duiwelsbos-waterfall.text: I have no idea why this route is marked 'easy', unless you're a mountain goat. Beautiful, yes, well-marked and maintained, certainly, worth it, absolutely, but easy it is not. The saving grace is that it is short! The ride to the start is good, though we bottomed out a few times. Definitely not great for low-cars!

duiwelsbos-waterfall.author: Laura Liebisch
duiwelsbos-waterfall.text: Nice hike through the Forrest, easy enough with few steep climbs. The start of the trail was challenging to find: Google maps is not helpful if you look for Duiwelsbos Hike. Instead search: Marloth Nature reserve. It will take you to the entrance of the reserve.

duiwelsbos-waterfall.author: yvonne kariuki
duiwelsbos-waterfall.text: The trail is beautiful 1 KM, easy hike. Done within an hour and a half at most going up this is stops for pics. Going down in +-40mins max on a very slow pace

tierkop.author: Agnes Nemet
tierkop.text: Besides the beautiful view that one can enjoy from the top, if you have chance to hike there in November you will be amazed to walk through a large field of Watsonias. Just a bit higher King Proteas will make your hike unforgettable. Worth the effort of climbing about 700 meters total ascent!

tierkop.author: Cebo wealthy
tierkop.text: It's beautiful, peaceful and worth the walk. When you're reach Tierkop, there are two gates, one leads to the gorge and the other to the Garden Route Dam, as well as other hiking trails.

tierkop.author: Jaco Roux
tierkop.text: Slept over in this hut years ago as part of a school hike. I just love the trail running in this area. Especially coming from the old George Dam side. I ran up the route recently and the views were beautiful as always. Unfortunately got some mist right at the top, but that is George for you.

terblans-nature-walk.author: Debbie Van Zyl
terblans-nature-walk.text: Beautiful hike. Not well marked at the finish, longer than 6,5km and definitely only for experienced hikers. Very steep incline towards the end and a very bad gravel road to get there. Lovely forest hike tho with picnic area.

terblans-nature-walk.author: Ernst Kamffer
terblans-nature-walk.text: What a beautiful hike in the Knysna forests. There are a few streams to cross which makes the hike so much fun for the children. Small children can easily make the hike if they are used to hiking. There is one part that is a moderate climb up with steps which took the children some time but at the end they still made it.

terblans-nature-walk.author: Fagmeedah Petersen
terblans-nature-walk.text: Beautiful walk through forest. Would not rate it as easy, like some of the guides. Best to wear trail shoes when wet. Definitely take the Gouna road from outside Simola - 10km of gravel road. If you take the Uniondale road, it is 35km of gravel.

green-mountain-trail.author: Shannon Drake
green-mountain-trail.text: What an incredible experience! We had a group of 20 ladies do the Green Mountain trail in October. Andreas and his team of guides are welcoming, knowledgeable, eager to share info and of course help along the trails.

green-mountain-trail.author: Charlotte
green-mountain-trail.text: Alison from Wildekrans Country House arranged a hike into the Fynbos with Andreas and Patrick for myself and my father and we had a brilliant time. We left fairly early in the morning to beat the summer heat but it was so worth it!

green-mountain-trail.author: Jacqui FRANCK
green-mountain-trail.text: I hiked the Green Mountain Trail from 15-17 October 2025. The trail begins and ends at the beautiful Wildekrans Country House each day. Friendly and knowledgeable guides Andries and Patrick met us at 7am each day and led us through vineyards, forests, and magnificent mountain paths with exquisite flowers and views and Eagles soaring above…

ou-kraal-hiking-trail.author: Mark Percy
ou-kraal-hiking-trail.text: Gently sloping balcony path with stunning views along the coast. For a great 5-mile circular walk, climb out of Kalk Bay on the Echo Valley Trail to Weary Willy. Descend to St James on the old mule trail. Drop down into town on the Westray Road steps.

ou-kraal-hiking-trail.author: Najuwah
ou-kraal-hiking-trail.text: Didn't go too far up my kids are afraid of heights. However, it is beautiful and peaceful up there. I would love to go all the way next time.

badskloof-trail.author: Michael Tucker
badskloof-trail.text: Trail is flooded I some places and not well maintained. Rock features are beautiful but you hike to Avalon springs. There are no actual natural springs to see

badskloof-trail.author: Jacques Cronjé
badskloof-trail.text: Check the weather before you try this one. It was raining the day before and it turned a greater part of the trail into a sticky, muddy marsh. I attempted it from both ends but could only get about 1km in before the trail was washed out.

badskloof-trail.author: Cheryl Theunissen
badskloof-trail.text: Parked and took a scramble up the rocky path to the gun turret on the top of Cogmanskloof. Our 3 year old managed up and down, which was great! Lively view from the top and quite clean, which was a pleasant surprise.

george-peak.author: Karla Strydom
george-peak.text: Such an amazing view and experience, of all my hikes so far - this Ill never forget! Please be prepaired to literally CLIMB a mountain, take your most supportive buddies aswell as water and food. Dont let this hike pass u by.

george-peak.author: Tyran Bester
george-peak.text: Absolutely incredible hike up to one of 6 peaks in the Outeniqua mountain range overshadowing the town of George. Hiking to the top of George Peak takes about 4 hours and can be slower coming back due to the steep and consistent decent. Take at least 2lt of water with for the hike.

george-peak.author: Aseza
george-peak.text: Lovely Experience.. Quite hectic to climb up there but indeed worth it.. Beautiful views.. Great for body fitness test 😊..

boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.author: Eric Cusick
boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.text: Road condition to furthest starting point (upper start route indicated by Google maps following gravel road) is not great so be carefully (from McGregor side). Otherwise a solid hike with a great rock pool along the way.

boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.author: JJ LR
boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.text: As a repeat visitor with friends and family this is one of my favorite hikes in beauty and convenience as it's not far from Cape Town and the booking is always uncomplicated and the owner very helpful .they offer to do your shopping at a nominal fee so no heavy packs to carry just overnight essentials and snacks.

boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.author: Asiphe Yakobi
boesmanskloof-hiking-trail.text: Booking was very easy n efficient. The trail itself was semi difficult 🤭 with breathtaking views 🗻 . It is very safe even though there were no field rangers on site. Oak tree waterfall is a beautiful stop...❤️ The trail is poorly maintained....most signage has fallen off the poles, you could possibly get lost.

van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.author: Zen Scoulelis
van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.text: Lovely spacious park with a river for water splashing, lots of picnic areas, tables and benches, shaded areas & lush grass areas. Ladel of Love sell organic veggies which supports their soup kitchen and there is a delightful coffee truck with great options & snacks

van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.author: Jannie Van Niekerk
van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.text: This park is always kept so clean by the city, amazing space

van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.author: MC Smit
van-riebeeck-park-hiking-trail.text: One of my favorite parks in Cape Town!

karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.author: Nielen Willem du Toit
karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.text: Walked from the quarry through to Worcester-West in Winter. Still very nice. Would suggest that spring summer will be a better time to go for a hike as the roads took some erosion damage due to the rains. Great views and options for roads to be taken. Make day of it.

karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.author: Brenda Loubser
karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.text: Stunning views on the hiking routes. Springtime ver special.

karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.author: Elize Buys
karoo-gardens-hiking-trails.text: Breathtaking views! Will definitely go again, just waiting for the flower season to start! There are 3 hiking trails, beautiful gardens and even a restaurant at the Karoo Gardens.

cradock-peak.author: Tyran Bester
cradock-peak.text: Absolutely incredible hike but very challenging. The hike from Witfontein to the top and back is about 18km with a 1600m ascent. Time it takes is around 10 hours if you include a few breaks. Enough water is a must. There is no accessible drinkable water on the mountain and many people suffer from dehydration.

cradock-peak.author: Leonex Ntando
cradock-peak.text: This is the best milestone any friends family. So many beautiful flowers to see . It is also thee best place to bond over pain . It is really far , but worth every meter on the trail .Absolutely breathtaking

cradock-peak.author: Joan Steenkamp
cradock-peak.text: Was an amazing experience climbing Cradock. You need to be somewhat fit as it's not an easy walk. You climb a lit and the legs are going to be sore. Also on coming down it is quite strenuous on the knees.

sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.author: Farai Mudzingwa
sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.text: I would classify it as medium level hiking trail. Great views, just some piece of advice for beginners, the trail has a couple of ups and downs and towards the end as you go back to the highway, there is a steep upward section, which may prove difficult for already tired legs so take it easy beforehand if you can.

sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.author: Anchelique Roerink
sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.text: It was a great hike. It's uphill most of the time and then coming back it's downhill so make sure you have comfortable shoes on. Mostly hiking in the sun with a few water crossings. Not much parking available. Make sure you take cash with as they dont accept card payments for entry.

sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.author: Leané van der Vyver
sleeping-beauty-day-mountain-hiking-trai.text: Even though there were times that I regretted my decision to go up that mountain... the view at the end was all worth it! There is no way to describe the feeling of being up there. I loved it!

cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.author: Tyran Bester
cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.text: The trail is stunning in its variety of plants and bird and wildlife. It is very exposed which makes it a hot trail to walk. Best would be early morning (before 9am) and late afternoon (after 3pm in winter or 6pm in summer) It gets tricky along the water due to Cape Mole Rat burrows and tunnels which collapse under your feet as you walk s…

cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.author: Ivan Poulter
cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.text: ⭐ 2/5 – “Cape Dune Mole-rat Trail? More like Cape Doom Sandtrap Trail!” If you’ve ever wanted to experience what it’s like to walk through a giant sandbox while being slowly roasted by the sun and attacked by mystery bushes, this is the trail for you. The sign promises “an easy and comfortable circular hike” — lies. All lies.

cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.author: Hey Demi Ann (Demi Ann)
cape-dune-mole-rat-trail-6-3-km-7-2-km.text: Lovely trail along the area around the beautiful dam. San parks always above and behind so clean and when marked ! I hope you enjoy this hike if your in the area. Felt safe all the way

secret-forest.author: Duncan Alfreds
secret-forest.text: This trail is part of the Klassenbosch trail but it really a lovely little hideaway forest in its own right and perfect for a walk removed from all the city madness, yet close enough to be super accessible (if you have you own transport).

secret-forest.author: Yash 00
secret-forest.text: Beautiful urban foresty walk following along a musical river stream. Intermittent shading under tall trees. Seating on logs or a bench to pause a while. Watch out for galloping horses and dog poo.

secret-forest.author: Juliet Drozdz
secret-forest.text: Wow what a beautiful walk and tucked away between residential homes. Plush surroundings, not busy like other trails. Joins up to the Alphen walk if you want to continue further

tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.author: Lizelle Gevers
tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.text: We did this 6-day hike in December 2025. It’s definitely one of the multi-day hikes that should be on everyone’s list. The trail is well-marked (except on D5 there could be a few more markers added) and the huts are in good condition. There is a donkey at every hut, so we had warm showers every day which is amazing!

tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.author: Riaz Lorgat
tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.text: An experience!! Forest trails differ and have their own identities. This one is really good. You could complete around 70km over the 5 days which could range from 3km a day to 22km. Basecamps are decent with some having donkey boilers for hot water, more then adequate sleeping and resting areas. It is the forest.

tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.author: TJ Morkel
tsitsikamma-hiking-trail.text: We did an overnight stay at Heuningbos hut. We used the emergency escape route up and back for a quick and relatively easy hike. Lots of fire wood and clean facilities. The hut was full of others hikers but it was good to have some privacy by the smaller teepee style hut.

salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.author: Rajesh Yandrathi
salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.text: This place is a stunning spot for a relaxing walk. The trail isn’t too challenging, and you might even be lucky enough to spot some Baboons, the local monkeys, along the way.

salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.author: Marit van Wijngaarden
salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.text: This place is really beautiful for a nice walk. The walk isn't too hard, and you might even get to see monkeys. The nature here looks like a jungle. It took us about three hours to finish walking the Nature Valley Loop.

salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.author: Tash Muller
salt-river-lookout-hiking-trail.text: We love doing this hike! The sound of nature, the smells, the peace, the views are spectacular! Highly recommend to take along your swimming gear and a good picnic and book and lounge around at the river / ocean for a few hours before trekking back.

3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.author: Paul Richard
3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.text: Such a beautiful, but hard trail! We did the Three Sisters hike, however it seemed like the sisters multiplied as you have to climb over no less than 6 hills and some of them are quite steep! It roughly takes between 3h30 and 4 hours to complete the circular route.

3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.author: Ashley Mark Lane
3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.text: Beautiful walking trail and exceptional views. Worth the effort to walk to the top for the amazing view.

3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.author: Wybrand Ganzevoort
3-sisters-jeans-hill-hiking-trail.text: Great hiking trail that passes through some amazing samples of cape fynbos. The hiking trail is quite clearly indicated and the trail has an easy to moderate difficulty level. The trail has been really well maintained with logs being added on a regular basis to stabalise the path.

hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.author: vernon singh
hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.text: If you think you got what it takes to hike this trail, then it's a must because this overnight hike will take everything you got. It's go time from the start, uphill all the way to to fantastic and well kept overnight hut. Definitely need a level of fitness, even so carrying all you stuff for the overnight adds to the struggle,lol.

hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.author: Richard Bolland
hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.text: An extremely beautiful but grueling hike. I found the descent way harder than the hike up. Day 1 highlights: A dip in the cold stream Walking past 30 different types of fynbos Amazing Rock formations Another dip in a pool. Seeing the hut! Braai, warm shower, great views.

hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.author: Ivan van der Merwe
hiking-dassieshoek-arangieskop-trails.text: The views and experience are some of the best I’ve had, and the "I’ve done it" feeling at the top is incredibly satisfying. I highly recommend Arangieskop, but don’t let this mountain fool you—it is a leg-killer. I’m a regular weightlifter and runner, and this pushed my limits.

oubaai-walking-trail.author: Gustav Erlank
oubaai-walking-trail.text: Great hike with several point to point options if you don't have the time or stamina for the loop. Enjoy hiking out at any point and cut through the golf estate back to the main entrance parking near the hotel. It's a good workout, up and downs. Fantastic views. Well maintained trail. Well laid out too.

oubaai-walking-trail.author: Justine Dreyer
oubaai-walking-trail.text: Mostly well maintained, except the section going down to the river, which doesn't seem to be a priority for them. A good variety of cliffside and forest walking with some amazing views. Goes along the perimeter of Oubaai all the way to the whale deck, but one can easily exit the trail at various points.

oubaai-walking-trail.author: Val O'Brien
oubaai-walking-trail.text: Difficult to find the start of the Trail but eventually we were directed to the Hotel and Spa car park (after signing a disclaimer). A very helpful Hotel employee took us to the start of the Trail (no signposts anywhere) steep downhill road until you reach the river, then up into the Bush (no turning back at this point) a variety of ups a…

geelbek-trail.author: Merle Nicholls
geelbek-trail.text: PERFECT SUNDAY OUTING - Did the 14km Strandveld hike. Absolutely spectacular. Saw approximately 40 Eland and a few smaller buck. Hike technical first half, Sandy and uphill until you get to Mile 16 beach. It is postcard beautiful. The hike back a walk in the park. Overall a lovely experience through Fynbos. I will recommend this walk.

geelbek-trail.author: canyoudoanybetter
geelbek-trail.text: I didn’t have the time to hike this. It looked like it would take a while to get all it and the wind was blowing very hard. I did see a nice group of Ostrich here! Buildings look quite historic.

geelbek-trail.author: andy wahl
geelbek-trail.text: Offering a lovely ride around the lagoon returning to food truck for coffee & or refreshments Geelbek restaurant remains closed tho

welvanpas-hiking-trail.author: André Goethals
welvanpas-hiking-trail.text: Beautiful hike with a lot of variety. From vineyards to deep dank forests, moss covered boulders, Pine Forest, meandering streams, lots of flowers. Literally 10 000s of mushrooms with a large number of different colors. The owner also gave me a map, which is the clearest and easiest to understand of any trail I've been on.

welvanpas-hiking-trail.author: Tania
welvanpas-hiking-trail.text: What a lovely place and such friendly hosts. The hike was perfect and not difficult except for the washed away path we took along the river. We were told to skip that path but somehow ended up going there. It’s doable if you go slow and really pretty. The views of the mountain ranges are stunning.

welvanpas-hiking-trail.author: Johann Janse Van Rensburg
welvanpas-hiking-trail.text: Such a nice hike, dead quiet, lots of shade for the hot summer days. The hosts are a pleasure to deal with, all in all a good experience.

power-stream-hiking-trail.author: Ina Von Brandis
power-stream-hiking-trail.text: A beautiful trail, hike lots of trees, forest and then little uphill. A wonderful experience in nature

power-stream-hiking-trail.author: Hennie Michaels
power-stream-hiking-trail.text: My playground whenever I need to test me against myself
